title: Omega Speedmaster 145.022-69ST Straight Writing with Original Box Papers and Extract of Archives
description: The Omega Speedmaster, launched in 1957, is arguably one of the most famous chronographs in history. Originally built for race car drivers, it accidentally became the ultimate tool for space exploration. Astronaut Wally Schirra wore his personal Speedmaster during the Mercury-Atlas 8 mission, and in 1965—after surviving brutal “torture tests,” including extreme heat, cold, shock, and vacuum—NASA officially flight-qualified the Speedmaster for all manned missions. Buzz Aldrin wore his Speedmaster (ST 105.012) on the lunar surface during Apollo 11, and it has been known as the “Moonwatch” ever since. Here we have a lovely reference 145.022, nicknamed the “Straight Writing.” This nickname refers to a specific engraving on the caseback of the Omega Speedmaster Professional Moonwatch produced in the early 1970s. It was Omega’s first commercial response to the 1969 moon landing, featuring the commemorative text “THE FIRST WATCH WORN ON THE MOON” engraved in horizontal straight lines rather than the curved (arched) text found on later models. The case on this specimen is in great condition, paired with a beautiful tachymeter bezel featuring the desirable “Dot Next to 90” (DNN90), free from major defects or scratches. The pie-pan–style dial is in equally nice condition, with warm citron-yellow lume and crisp white printed text. The watch comes fitted with a Bulang and sons leather strap. Furthermore, the watch comes with its original Omega box, a hangtag, the guarantee booklet (stating a sale in Nottingham, UK) with its red plastic holder, and an Omega Extract of the Archives confirming production in 1971 and delivery to the UK. The manual-wind Caliber 861 has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready for many years of enjoyment. We do have a load of options for dressing a Speedmaster up- or down , you might want to check our strap guide over HERE We think these Speedmasters are among the most iconic and best-looking chronographs ever made, but it is becoming increasingly difficult to find examples in such honest and well-preserved condition. This one certainly rows our boat—get in touch if it rows yours too. Specifications: Omega Reference 145.022-69 ST Speedmaster professional 42mm steel case with straight writing case back Movement Serial 313xxxxx dating to 1971 Pie pan style dial with yellow lume Faded to dark grey DNN 90 bezel Comes on Bulang and sons leather strap Caliber 861 hand wound movement Omega box, hangtag , guarantee booklet Omega Extract of the Archives confirming production in 1971 and delivery to the UK Fully serviced by our watchmaker 1 year mechanical warranty Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Omega Cosmic 2486-2 Jumbo Black Farsi Numerals Dial Pink Gold Plated
description: The Omega Cosmic Ref. 2486 represents one of the brand’s most intriguing yet quietly underappreciated achievements of the mid-20th century. Featuring a full calendar complication, it once stood among the most sophisticated offerings in Omega’s lineup, reflecting the technical ambition of the era. Calendar-equipped Cosmics often fly under the radar, frequently overshadowed by more complex perpetual calendar models. Yet their charm lies precisely in this balance of practicality, elegance, and mechanical interest. This particular example elevates the reference even further, distinguished by an exceptionally rare dial configuration with day and month displays rendered in Farsi (Persian)—a detail seldom encountered and highly desirable among seasoned collectors. Compact in concept yet rich in character, this watch offers the appeal of a calendar complication without the ostentation often associated with larger, more imposing counterparts. A refined and culturally significant piece, it stands as a fascinating alternative to the more celebrated calendar watches of its time. Farsi-dial Omega Cosmics are a recognized category among collectors, yet they were not bespoke commissions. Instead, they originated from a very limited production run intended for the Middle Eastern market. A closer look at the dial reveals an important distinction: rather than the Eastern Arabic numerals commonly seen today, the script is true Farsi, identifiable by the heart-shaped numeral five. Most of these watches were originally delivered to Iran. It is widely believed that the majority were sold in Tehran, then a rapidly modernizing, oil-wealthy capital under the rule of Mohammad Reza Pahlavi. The Omega Cosmic calendar was produced in five round-case references, offered in both 35mm (Refs. 2606, 2471, and 2473) and larger 37mm formats (Refs. 2485 and 2486). Farsi-signed dials are most frequently encountered in the 2606 and 2486 references. The present example features a “jumbo” 37.5mm pink gold-plated case—an era-specific detail that highlights Omega’s broad material offerings at the time, which ranged from stainless steel to gold-plated and solid gold constructions. The pink gold-plated case is in lovely condition and features a snap-back case back, side correctors for the moon phase and calendar functions, elegant lugs, and a thin bezel, allowing the dial to appear even larger on the wrist. The black dial shows some honest age, yet remains highly attractive and sympathetic for a watch dating to the late 1940s. It features the aforementioned Farsi numerals, with matching Farsi calendar displays visible through the dial apertures. Additional details include leaf-shaped hands, a blued hand for the date indication, a small running seconds at six o’clock, and a beautifully rendered moonphase in the same area. We have paired this Omega with a fine Bulang & Sons leather strap. The watch is powered by the manually wound Cal. 381, based on a Lemania ébauche, and has been fully servi

title: Patek Philippe Annual Calendar 5035P Black Dial in Platinum with Box
description: Patek Philippe revolutionized horology in 1996 by introducing the Annual Calendar with Ref. 5035, a patented “useful complication” that requires adjustment only once a year in February. Designed as a more accessible alternative to the intricate perpetual calendar, this innovation quickly became a cornerstone of the brand’s collection and has since appeared in numerous, diverse designs. We currently have a Ref. 5035P — the “P” denoting a platinum case. This standout watch features a 37mm platinum case with a slooped bezel, correctors on the flanks, and a sapphire case back that reveals the beautifully hand-finished movement. The black dial houses subdials for the calendar functions in English, a date cutout, and a 24-hour sub dial at six o’clock. Applied Roman numeral hour markers contain tritium lume, complemented by elegant leaf hands with luminova. The watch comes on its original Patek Philippe leather strap with a matching platinum folding clasp. Original inner and outer Patek Philippe boxes are also included. The movement (cal. 135) has been fully checked and regulated by our watchmaker, ensuring precise performance. A combination of elegance, technical innovation, and collectible value — Enquire now to make this platinum Annual Calendar yours! title: Patek Philippe Calatrava 18k White Gold Clous de Paris ref. 5120G
description: The Patek Philippe Calatrava, launched in 1932 with the iconic Reference 96, is a legendary collection symbolizing timeless, minimalist elegance. It emerged after Patek Philippe’s acquisition by the Stern brothers during the Great Depression—a pivotal moment that steered the brand toward understated, high-quality design. Named after the brand’s cross emblem—originally linked to a medieval Spanish chivalric order—the Calatrava set the standard for the modern dress watch with its clean lines, round case, and Bauhaus-inspired “form follows function” aesthetic. The Reference 5120 is a spiritual successor to the beloved hobnail (“clous de Paris”) Calatrava models such as the manual-wind Ref. 3919, translating that classic design language into an elegant automatic format. This ultra-thin 18k white gold case, measuring 35 mm, is in lovely condition, with its double row of hobnail pyramids around the bezel still crisp and well defined. It features gently curved lugs and a sapphire display back that offers a clear view of the 22k gold micro-rotor and the beautifully finished Caliber 240—an icon in its own right. The dial presents a clean white canvas with painted Roman numerals, no seconds hand for an added sense of tranquility, and simple blackened stick hands. It is paired with a refined Bulang & Sons leather strap and fitted with an 18k white gold Patek Philippe pin buckle. This minimalistic Calatrava has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready to be enjoyed for many years to come. The 5120 represents perhaps the last generation of truly two-hand (hour + minute) automatic Calatavas from Patek — minimalism at its finest.. Give your collection the clous de Paris touch it deserves—take this refined Calatrava home! Specifications: Patek Philippe Calatrava Reference 5120G 35mm 18k white gold case with “hobnail” bezel Display case back White dial with black Roman numerals and non-lumed hands Bulang and sons leather strap , 18 k white gold Patek' pin buckle Calibre 240 automatic movement with gold micro rotor Movement number 120xxxx, dating to the early 2000’s Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Rolex Cosmograph Daytona 6262 Exotic White Dial
description: One of the most iconic wristwatch models in history is the Rolex Daytona. It occupies an ultra-rare status among vintage Rolex collectors, often regarded as the ultimate grail. The entire line of 4-digit Daytona references is celebrated for their rarity, beauty, and collectibility, with a particular dial layout known as the “grail”- the so-called exotic dials ! Many enthusiasts are already familiar with the story of the Daytona. Originally introduced under the name Cosmograph “Le Mans,” it was later renamed “Daytona” after the famous racetrack in Florida. Initially, the model did not experience overwhelming popularity; it was a unpopular watch with worldwide modest interest. To boost its appeal and capture the aesthetic of the era across multiple model lines, Rolex commissioned their dial manufacturer, Singer, to develop a design study for potential new dials. These early variations, featured in Singer catalogs, included the first prototypes for the “Exotic” dials, bolder, with a 1960s vibe, vivid colors, Art Deco fonts, and more intriguing details. At the time of their release in the 1960s, these dials were considered quirky and not highly sought after compared to other Rolex models. However, several key factors eventually contributed to their rise in popularity… This specific one is a rare reference 6262 and represents the second generation of the Daytona to feature the coveted 'Exotic' dial, and it was produced for just a single year—between ca 1970 and 1971. While it may appear nearly identical to its predecessor on the outside, several notable changes were made internally. Most significantly, it houses the upgraded Valjoux 727 movement. Unlike the earlier Ref. 6239, which used the Valjoux 722 ticking at 18,000 vibrations per hour, the 727 operates at a faster rate of 21,600 vibrations per hour, offering improved precision and performance. The case is in great condition , with a lovely steel”unites per hour “ bezel , pump style pushers and a nice Oyster riveted expandable bracelet ref 6635 fitted, with end links marked ‘57’ and a clasp stamped 2.68 . The eggshell like white “exotic” dial is in lovely condition with yellow colour lume , and a contrasting black hour track .The hands are later to the watch as they feature luminova lume. This lovelyDaytona has been fully serviced by our watch maker. These watches are every collectors “chase “ and side effects include staring at your wrist for unreasonable lengths of time , get in touch with us ! Specifications Rolex Cosmograph Daytona 6262 36mm steel case with plexi glass Serial 262xxxx dating to 1971 “Pump “pushers White “Exotic” step-dial in with black accents and prints Steel tachymeter “units per hour “bezel Rolex riveted Oyster bracelet ref 6635 with end links 57 Manual wound cal. 727 based on a valjoux 72 caliber Fully serviced by our watchmaker 1 year mechanical warranty Fully insured delivery

title: Audemars Piguet Disco volante in 18 k yellow gold

Sometimes we tend to forget that AP also created high complications and gorgeous dress watches long before the mighty ‘Oak’ defined the lineup. Audemars Piguet worked with independent designers, such as the German Gebhard Duve, who designed the so-called “Disco Volante.” On offer, we have a super cool, ultra-thin, and rare “Disco Volante,” or “Flying Saucer” dress watch, in an 18k yellow gold, 31mm case. The case features very short lugs, a small winding crown, and a machined, concentric bezel—simply super cool. The dial is silvered, with simple stick markers, a small running seconds, elegant Audemars Piguet signature, and hands that perfectly match the hour markers. This fine Disco Volante is fitted with a Bulang & Sons leather strap, and its ultra-thin, manually wound Cal. 2003 movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. If you’re looking for a truly unique, ultra-thin dress watch that embodies elegance and rarity, this Disco Volante is ready to be on your wrist—light, stylish, and unforgettable. Get in touch to make it yours ! title: Rolex Oyster Perpetual 1002 Silver Soleil Swiss Only Dial in 18 k yellow gold Coca Cola
description: The Rolex Oyster Perpetual is the foundational pillar of the brand, combining the two most significant innovations in horological history: the waterproof Oyster case and the self-winding Perpetual movement. The name “Oyster Perpetual” was officially codified to represent the union of these two technologies in a single, cohesive model. At hand we have a lovely early 1960s reference 1002, presented in a 34 mm 18k yellow gold execution with a smooth bezel, all in beautiful condition. A particularly charming and unique detail is the engraving on the case back: “1937–1962 Coca-Cola” commemorating the 25-year jubilee of Mr. H. Kirchfeld for the German branch of the Coca-Cola Company. This gives the piece a wonderful sense of history and an added layer of character. The dial is equally attractive, featuring a warm silver soleil finish, elegant hour markers, a “Swiss” only designation at six o’clock, and fine dauphine hands. Fitted with a leather strap with a Rolex plaque buckle, and fully serviced, this elegant dress watch is ready to be enjoyed for many years to come. This really is what watch collecting is about, finding the rare ones in good condition with a great story . Please do not hesitate to ask us about it. We are glad to help! title: Patek Philippe Calatrava 5107G Silver dial Box and Paper Set in 18k White Gold sold by Wempe

It seamlessly bridges traditional elegance with contemporary durability, featuring a screw-down crown and sapphire display back. Notably, the 5107 introduced integrated crown guards to the Calatrava family, lending a slightly sportier, more robust feel while maintaining its timeless dress watch proportions. The watch on offer is a Ref. 5107G, with the ‘G’ denoting an 18k white gold case. The case is in fantastic condition, showcasing the see-through case back that offers a full view of the automatic Calibre 315 SC with its gold rotor. The silver dial features a distinctive “dot-dot” minute track, boat-shaped markers, dauphine hands, and a date function, combining legibility with understated elegance. This Calatrava comes paired with a Bulang & Sons leather strap and the original Patek 18k white gold pin buckle. It is further accompanied by its original inner and outer boxes, leather paper holder, booklets, original Wempe invoice, warranty card, and certificate of origin, documenting its sale in Germany in 2004.The watch and its cal.315 SC movement have been fully checked and regulated by our watch maker. Experience the refined elegance of the Calatrava 5107—where classic dress watch sophistication meets a subtly sportier, modern edge ! title: Omega Seamaster 300 British Royal Navy Issued ref. 165.024 Mil-spec Circle-T Dial
description: The Omega Seamaster 300 has a storied military heritage, most notably with the British Royal Navy, which commissioned a special version in 1967. This military issue was a modified Seamaster 300, featuring fixed lugs for a one-piece strap, an encircled “T” to denote tritium lume, and a large triangular marker at 12 o’clock for improved readability. These military watches, delivered between 1967 and 1971, were engraved with service identifiers such as “0552” for the Royal Navy or “W10” for the Army. The “Big Triangle” dial, paired with the broad sword hands, offers exceptional legibility and now displays a beautiful yellow patina on the lume. The use of tritium on the hands and indices is confirmed by the circled “T” on the dial — a hallmark reserved for true military dive watches. The bezel remains in excellent condition, showing no major defects or cracks. The dial features a sharp, proud encircled “T,” the correct gladiator-style handset, and a superb yellow-colored lume patina. The case back features the “broad arrow” (or “pheon”), a small upward-pointing arrow indicating British Crown property. It is engraved with “0552,” confirming its Royal Navy issue. The watch is powered by an automatic Caliber 552, which has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. It is fitted with a one-pass strap, as originally used. It comes with a handwritten card, stamped and signed by an Omega boutique in Zürich, Switzerland, denoting: “all original parts / not restored military ‘T’ dial” — dated 2024. Inside beats the automatic, red-gilded Omega Caliber 552 movement, fully serviced and ready for its next mission. This Omega Seamaster 300 is a true military classic — as collectible as it is wearable. Add a rare and historic Omega to your collection today ! title: Audemars Piguet Ultra Thin Dress Watch in 18kt White Gold Silver Diamond Markers Dial
description: The history of Audemars Piguet (AP) is a story of independence and high-end innovation. Founded in 1875, it remains the oldest fine watchmaking manufacturer still in the hands of its founding families—the Audemars and Piguet families. Jules Louis Audemars and Edward Auguste Piguet joined forces in Le Brassus, Switzerland. Audemars focused on creating complex movements, while Piguet handled the final finishing and commercial operations. The brand soon gained fame for miniaturizing complex mechanisms, and in 1892 they developed the world’s first minute repeater wristwatch. Facing the Quartz Crisis in the 1970s, AP took a bold gamble by hiring designer Gérald Genta to create a luxury watch made of steel rather than gold. The result was the iconic Audemars Piguet Royal Oak, with its octagonal bezel and visible screws—ultimately saving the company and creating the luxury steel sports watch category. Together with Vacheron Constantin and Patek Philippe, AP forms part of the so-called “Holy Trinity” of watchmaking. Yet unlike its peers, AP is often considered the most rebellious of the three, blending traditional craftsmanship with avant-garde designs and bold collaborations. This lovely dress watch represents one of the “pre-Royal Oak” specialties of AP: the ultra-thin watches. During the mid-20th century, several watchmakers pushed the boundaries of how thin a mechanical movement could be, allowing watches to become more elegant and lightweight—a “competition” in which AP was a true leader. Here we have a beautiful example: an ultra-thin 18k white gold “time-only” dress watch measuring 31 mm, with small lugs and a delicate thin bezel. The silvered dial features 12 diamond hour markers and elegant stick hands. Without a running seconds hand, the dial offers a certain sense of calm and serenity. This elegant piece is paired with a beautiful Bulang & Sons leather strap, and its ultra-thin movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. Interested in this beautiful pre-Audemars Piguet Royal Oak era dress watch? We’re happy to share more details. Specifications: Audemars Piguet dress watch “Ultra Thin” 31mm 18k white gold case Case number 310xx / movement no 93xxx dating to the late 1950’s Silver dial with Diamond set hour markers , stick hands Snap back case back Ultra thin hand wound movement Comes on the pictured Bulang and sons leather strap Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

This Oyster Perpetual Date is in great condition and features a striking soleil-finished blue dial with a very distinctive effect. Around the stick hour markers—where the zinc sulfide / tritium lume is situated—the blue takes on an “exploding” appearance. This effect is characteristic of blue dials from the era and can be seen not only on Rolex, but occasionally on Patek Philippe and IWC as well. As the patina is evenly present around all markers, we find this dial particularly unique, which is exactly why we decided to buy and offer this piece for someone seeking a watch with real character. The watch has been fully checked and regulated by our watchmaker and is ready to be enjoyed. Proof that character beats size every time.Blue, bold, and exploding with character—this unique Oyster is waiting for you ! title: Cartier Tank Obus CPCP in 18 k yellow gold ref 2380
description: The Cartier Tank Obus was introduced in 1923 and has only ever been produced in extremely limited numbers. The model stands apart thanks to its distinctive case design. Unlike its rectangular Tank counterparts, the Obus features a square case with charming, bullet-shaped lugs—hence the name “Obus,” French for bullet . This rare Collection Privée Cartier Paris (CPCP) Tank Obus is crafted in 18k yellow gold and measures 26.5 × 33.5 mm. The silver dial is beautifully finished with fine guilloché engraving and features painted black Roman numerals paired with elegant blued Breguet hands. True to Cartier tradition, the winding crown is adorned with a blue sapphire cabochon. Turning the watch over reveals a display case back showcasing the manual-winding movement, with attractively finished plates decorated with Cartier’s signature interlocking double “C” motif. Fitted with a refined Bulang & Sons strap, this Tank Obus is a supremely elegant dress watch with a distinctly luxury presence. Powering the watch is the hand-wound caliber 430 C, based on a Piaget movement. It has been fully checked and regulated by our watchmaker and is ready to be enjoyed for many years to come. A true pièce de collection —sharp, rare, and unmistakably Cartier. If you’re aiming to add a little Obus to your collection, this is a shot you won’t want to miss! description: The Omega Seamaster 300 has a storied military heritage, most notably with the British Royal Navy, which commissioned a special version in 1967. This military issue was a modified Seamaster 300 featuring fixed lugs for a one-piece strap, an encircled “T” to denote tritium lume, and a large triangular marker at 12 o’clock for improved readability. These military watches, delivered between 1967 and 1971, were engraved with service identifiers such as “0552” for the Royal Navy or “W10” for the Army. The “Big Triangle” dial, paired with the broad sword hands, offers exceptional legibility and now displays a beautiful yellow patina on the lume. The use of tritium on the hands and indices is confirmed by the circled “T” on the dial — a hallmark reserved for true military dive watches. The bezel remains in excellent condition, showing no major defects or cracks. This exceptional tool watch is fitted with one of our Bulang and Sons single-pass nylon straps. Over the years, the case lost its original fixed bars — likely after its military service — perhaps to allow for the fitting of a metal bracelet. The caseback bears the military code “0552/923-7xxx A/347/70,” confirming delivery to the Royal Navy in 1970. Inside beats the automatic, red-gilded Omega Caliber 552 movement, which has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. Ready for its next mission, this Omega Seamaster 300 is a true military classic — as collectible as it is wearable. Built for men who don’t flinch at 50 metres under the Sea level and issued to those who actually earned the right to wear it — the Omega “Big Triangle” Seamaster isn’t just a handsome face, it’s proper military kit . Service-issued, battle-ready, and tough as they come. Don’t stall — a Royal Navy Seamaster doesn’t wait for stragglers. ⚓ Specifications: Omega Seamaster 300 165.024 British Royal Navy issued 42mm steel case wit twisted lugs and Case-back engraved “0552/923-7xxx A/347/70,” per MOD Specifications Arabic 12-6-9 layout, Circle-T dial Sword hands Calibre 552 automatic movement Bulang and sons single pass nylon strap Movement Serial 293xxxxx dating to 1968 Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty

title: Rolex Submariner non serif dial ref 5512
description: The Rolex Submariner 5512, introduced in 1959, was a pivotal reference that helped define the modern Submariner, most notably through the introduction of crown guards—a first for the model line. Produced alongside its non–chronometer-rated sibling, the 5513, for roughly two decades, the 5512 set itself apart with its chronometer-certified movement. Over its long production run, the reference saw several noteworthy evolutions, including changes to the crown guards (from early “square” to later “eagle beak” forms) and dial transitions from gilt to matte and, eventually, “Maxi” styles. This example dates to 1977 and features a matte black, so-called “non-serif” dial. The case remains strong and well proportioned, and it is fitted with a slightly faded fat-font bezel insert. The dial shows a beautiful yellow patina on the hour plots, giving the watch that extra bit of vintage charm. It comes with a solid-link Rolex Oyster bracelet, reference 93150, with 580 end links and a clasp stamped V1 / X1. The chronometer-rated movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. We think the no-date 5512 remains something of a sleeper—rare, desirable, and increasingly appreciated. If there’s one watch we’ll always have a soft spot for, it’s the Submariner no -date. Let us know what you’re looking for — we’d be happy to help you find the perfect example. title: Rolex Submariner Small Crown ref. 5508 T<25 Gilt Dial
description: As you might know by now — we love Submariners. Whether it’s a contemporary version with a gloss dial and sapphire crystal, or a vintage plexi piece with matte tritium dials — maxi dials, meters-first, or red Subs — we get warm, fuzzy feelings for all of them! The early versions without crown guards might just be the most distinctive of all. The so-called “Big Crowns” have an unmistakably bold presence on the wrist — that chunky case profile, the domed plexi sitting high above the dial — all combining to give a wonderfully rugged, oversized feel. On the other hand, the “Small Crown” references feel more refined, sitting flatter on the wrist — a different experience altogether, but equally cool in our book. At hand, we have just that: an early Submariner “Small Crown,” reference 5508. Take a look at the case on this one — especially the factory finish along the sides. We rarely use the word unpolished , but in this case… these hips don’t lie, baby! This example dates to 1959, so it would have originally been born with a “Swiss only” dial. The current dial, however, is a 1960s replacement, signed Swiss <T-25 . We presume the watch went back to Rolex for service sometime in the mid-1960s and received a new dial at that time — as many did. After all, these watches were used , not pampered! By then, Rolex had already moved away from the earlier luminous materials of the 1950s, which had been restricted under Swiss law. Nevertheless, this dial remains a beautiful gilt example — instantly recognizable for its glossy, mirror-like surface and warm golden printing. The term “gilt” doesn’t refer to gold leaf, but rather to the exposed brass base revealed after layers of black lacquer were applied and carefully etched away. Because of the complexity and cost of this process, Rolex eventually switched to simpler pad printing. Yet these gilt dials possess a charm that only time can create — aging with warmth and individuality, just like the one here, even if it’s a few years younger than the case itself. Overall, this watch is in superb condition (again — look at that case!). The insert is a later addition, but beautifully matched, the dial with gilt text in a light golden hue and luminous material that’s aged to a lovely lemon tone. Another not to mention is the Submariner writing ABOVE the depth rate -this is SUPER rare , we did not recall we have seen many others ! It’s fitted with a stretch riveted bracelet, reference 6636 — what’s not to love? Fully serviced by our watchmaker and ready for new adventures, this cool tool watch has all the charm you’d hope for. So, if you’re after a fantastic case, a rich gilt glow, and a dial full of character — without the fragility of 1950s lume — this might just be the one . Specifications: Rolex Reference 5508 Submariner “Small crown “ 38mm steel case Serial 399xxx dating to 1959 Superglossy”service “ gilt dial , signed “Swiss T<-25, , tritium zinc sulfide lume, Rare “Submariner over depth” Fat font bezel insert

title: Patek Philippe Golden Ellipse Porcelain dial in 18k yellow gold ref 3844J
description: Two thousand years ago, Euclid identified an enduring principle of aesthetic proportion known as the Golden Section. So perfect are its proportions that the Renaissance mathematician Luca Pacioli later named it De Divina Proportione —the ratio of 1 to 1.618, defining the precise point at which a line may be divided into two harmonious yet unequal lengths. Almost sixty years ago, Patek Philippe embraced this same principle of balance and elegance in the creation of the Golden Ellipse, a design that remains entirely unique within the brand’s portfolio. Presented here is a “TV-shaped” Golden Ellipse, reference 3844, crafted in 18k yellow gold. Measuring 33 x 33 mm, the case features short, elegant lugs and a discreet winding crown, resulting in a refined and perfectly balanced presence on the wrist. The porcelain dial is rich in detail and character. It features a delicate dotted - applied minute track, raised black enamel Patek Philippe signature, and sigma markings at six o’clock, indicating that the applied hour markers and sharp dauphine hands are executed in solid gold. Together, these elements create a dial of remarkable depth and quiet sophistication. The watch is paired with a beautiful Bulang & Sons leather strap . Both the watch and its hand-wound Caliber 215 have been fully serviced by our watchmaker, ensuring reliable performance for years to come. A watch guided not by trends, but by timeless proportion. An effortlessly cool dress watch — ready to be wrapped around your wrist. title: Rolex Datejust 16220 Khanjar Case-back, Khanjar Box and Paper Set
description: If you have spent any serious time around vintage watches, you have likely noticed that some dials feature more than just a brand signature and model name. From time to time, an unexpected emblem appears — a crest, a logo, or even a national symbol — transforming an already desirable timepiece into something layered with historical and political significance. These special-order pieces often surface at leading dealers or headline major auction catalogs, where they consistently spark strong interest among collectors. One of the most recognized of these emblems is the Khanjar of Oman — the traditional curved dagger set before crossed swords. Adopted as a national symbol in 1970, it became closely associated with the reign of Sultan Qaboos bin Said al Said. During his five-decade rule, which lasted until 2020, Oman developed into a stable and internationally respected nation. Watches bearing the Khanjar reflect the unique relationship between the Sultan and some of Switzerland’s most prestigious manufactures. Often commissioned as presentation pieces or official gifts, they represent a fascinating intersection of horology, diplomacy, and modern Middle Eastern history. At a certain point, Rolex decided to discontinue the production of custom-printed dials. As a result, the Khanjar emblem moved from the dial to the case-back. At hand we have a classic and ever-relevant Datejust, reference 16220, housed in a timeless 36 mm stainless steel case with a beautifully machined steel bezel. The case remains strong and well-defined, retaining its proportions nicely, and the engraved Khanjar on the case back is still exceptionally crisp and sharp. The elegant silver soleil dial is in excellent condition, featuring black printed text with refined serifs. A charming railway minute track with small Roman numerals adds a subtle touch of sophistication. The watch is fitted with a Jubilee bracelet, reference 62150H, with end links marked 555B and a clasp stamped U10. Included in the sale are the inner and outer Rolex boxes with crest, three booklets, and a special envelope printed with the Khanjar emblem and the text: “With the compliments of Qaboos Bin Saïd, Sultan of Oman”and the Punched paper stating sale through AD Khimji Ramdas Mascate. The watch and its quickset movement have been fully serviced by our watchmaker. A wonderful opportunity to acquire a historically significant and highly collectible Datejust — understated at first glance, yet rich in meaning and provenance. Specifications: Rolex Reference 16220 Datejust 36mm steel case, Sapphire crystal Steel engine turned bezel Serial a16xxxx dating to 1999 Silver soleil dial with black printed text Quick set movement “Khanjar” engraved on the case-back Jubilee bracelet 62150H with 555b end links Rolex inner and outer boxes, 3 booklets, envelope printed with the Khanjar emblem Punched warrantee paper stating sale through AD Khimji Ramdas, Mascate Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free

title: Patek Philippe Retrograde Perpetual Calendar 5059G Officer Case in 18k White Gold with Original Certificate of Origine
description: Introduced in 1998, Reference 5059 was the first serially produced perpetual calendar wristwatch with a retrograde date ever made by Patek Philippe. It was also the first perpetual calendar model since the legendary References 2497 and 2438-1 to feature a sweep centre-seconds hand. The watch is distinguished by its 36 mm officier-style case, with elongated lugs secured by screwed bars, as well as a hinged case back incorporating a display window and correctors for the moon-phase and calendar functions. Powering the watch is the automatic Calibre 315, fitted with a 21-carat gold rotor. The white dial features black Roman numerals as hour markers, cut-outs for the perpetual calendar and moon-phase displays, and is topped with four elegant blackened hands. The captivating retrograde date is fully integrated into the perpetual calendar mechanism, with the date hand instantly “flying” back to 1 at the end of each month. This fine complication is fitted with an original Patek Philippe leather strap and an 18K white-gold Calatrava style folding clasp. Furthermore the watch is accompanied by its original certificate of origin, stating a sale in 2004, as well as a 2025 service invoice from an authorized Patek Philippe dealer. As a result, the watch remains under Patek Philippe warranty. Total production of the Ref. 5059 remained under 1,400 pieces, spanning 18K yellow, white, and rose gold, along with platinum. Notably, fewer than 400 white-gold examples were produced throughout its eight-year lifespan.. Retrograde, rare, conversation starter and ridiculously charming—just like you! Specifications: Patek Philippe Perpetual Calendar reference 5059G 36mm 18k white gold case with display/ hinged case-back Perpetual Calendar retrograde layout with moon phase, English language Automatic movement Cal.315 with 21k gold-rotor Movement no 323xxxx case no 421xxxx dating to the early 2000’s Patek Philippe leather strap with original Patek Philippe 18k white gold folding buckle Patek Philippe certificate of origin stating sale in 2004 2025 Patek AD service invoice Still under Patek' warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Breguet Classique 7147 Silver Guilloché Dial in 18k Rose Gold
description: Breguet is one of the world’s oldest and most prestigious watch manufactures, founded in 1775 by the legendary Abraham-Louis Breguet in Paris. Often referred to as the father of modern watchmaking, his inventions and design codes continue to shape the industry to this day. The reference 7147 draws direct inspiration from the 18th- and 19th-century masterpieces of Abraham-Louis Breguet. It features the signature fluted coin-edge case in 18k rose gold, measuring 40 mm in diameter and an impressively slim 6 mm in thickness — elegant, refined, and unmistakably Breguet.the case has a display case back fitted with a full view on the hand finished movement . The dial is a showcase of traditional craftsmanship. It features a wider chapter ring for the hour numerals, broader-grain hobnail engine-turning at the centre, and a beautifully executed crosshatch guilloché on the seconds sub-dial set at a 45-degree angle — creating a truly striking visual effect. In a historical nod to the brand’s fight against counterfeiting — a practice dating back to 1795 — the 7147 incorporates a discreet “secret signature” engraved on the dial near 12 o’clock. The dial is completed with Breguet’s iconic blued steel open-tipped hands, another enduring house signature. Powering the watch is the ultra-thin automatic calibre 502.3 SD, derived from the renowned Frédéric Piguet calibre 71 architecture of the 1970s — a movement celebrated for its slim profile and reliability. The watch is fitted with a beautiful Bulang & Sons leather strap and has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. A wonderfully elegant, classically inspired timepiece that perfectly reflects Breguet’s historic design language. Feel free to get in touch for further details or to arrange a viewing. Specifications: Breguet Classique reference 7147 40 mm 18k Rose gold , coin-edge case -with display case back Serial 39xx dating to circa 2016 Silvered Guilloché Dial with blued Breguet hands and small running seconds Ultra-thin automatic calibre 502.3 SD, derived from the Frédéric Piguet cal.71 Bulang and sons leather strap Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Patek Philippe Golden Ellipse Blue Jeans Dial Ref. 3534-2 in 18k Yellow Gold
description: The Patek Philippe Golden Ellipse has been in production since 1968. It was introduced as a completely new concept, based on the “golden ratio” — which is where it gets its name. The elliptical case proportions were designed according to this mathematical principle, creating a perfectly balanced and timeless form. While the Golden Ellipse is most commonly seen in its vertical form, this example is a rare horizontal version, featuring the typical lugs for a gold Ellipse, in a 32 x 27 mm 18k yellow gold case. The star of this watch is undoubtedly the dial. Originally born blue (the base is actually 18k gold!), it features a diagonal engraved finish, which has beautifully aged into a stunning “jeans” effect — reminiscent of your favorite pair of denim after years of wear and washing. We absolutely love this character. We paired this “Jeans” Ellipse with a blue Bulang & Sons leather strap, equipped with an 18k gold Patek Philippe Ellipse buckle. The watch and its hand-wound Cal. 175 movement have been fully serviced by our watchmaker. This Golden Ellipse is not only an elegant dress watch — it’s a watch with personality. The dial gives it character, style, and an unmistakable edge. Let’s get this Jeans PP on your wrist! title: Rolex Oyster Perpetual 6581 Gilt OCC Dial
description: The Rolex Oyster Perpetual collection was officially introduced in 1931, marking a pivotal moment in watchmaking history. It debuted the revolutionary Rolex Perpetual movement — a self-winding mechanism that harnesses the natural motion of the wearer’s wrist to power the watch. This innovation eliminated the need for manual winding, delivering a new level of everyday convenience and reliability. Paired with Rolex’s iconic Oyster case, first launched in 1926 as the world’s first waterproof watch case, the Perpetual movement formed a truly groundbreaking combination. The result was the Oyster Perpetual: a timepiece defined by durability, precision, and practicality. With its self-winding movement and robust waterproof construction, it quickly established itself as the ideal everyday watch and further cemented Rolex’s status as a pioneer of horological innovation. This mid-1950s reference 6581 is executed in a lovely 34mm case with a steel engine-turned bezel. The dial variations on these have become a collecting world in themselves — from Explorer-style dials to black and white honeycomb versions. At hand, we have a high-gloss gilt dial with elegant markers, a “Swiss Only” designation at six o’clock, and a rare silver-printed “Officially Certified Chronometer” inscription on the lower half of the dial, topped off with dauphine hands and mustard-colored lume plots. The watch is paired with a Bulang & Sons straight end-link bracelet — a super-cool combination, in our view — and has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. A true mid-century classic — inquire now to make this rare gilt-dial 6581 yours. Specifications: - Rolex Reference 6581 Oyster Perpetual - 34mm steel case - Steel engine-turned bezel - Black gilt dial, OCC writing, signed “Swiss Only,” and dauphine hands - Serial 466xx dating to 1954 - Steel Bulang & Sons straight end-link bracelet - Fully serviced by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Patek Philippe Annual Calendar Flyback Chronograph 5960P in Platinum Execution Grey dial Full Set
description: The first of a new generation chronographs After an extended period of Patek Philippe producing calendar watches with Valjoux or Lemania base movements, the brand made a significant leap in 2006 with the introduction of a groundbreaking new reference and complication that highlighted their in-house movement within a calendar chronograph. This innovation led to the creation of the elegant yet sporty 5960 in Platinum, an annual calendar “flyback” chronograph. The annual calendar, originally introduced by Patek Philippe in 1996 with reference 5035, offers the functionality of a perpetual calendar but at a lower cost, to make a bridge between a basic calatrava and perpetual. It offers than the same character as the more mature perpetual but with the require to set it manually only once a year in February. With the 5960, Patek Philippe advanced this complication by incorporating a chronograph module, providing a modern alternative to their traditional perpetual chronographs like the 5970/5270. This marked the debut of an in-house chronograph movement and set the stage alongside the Nautilus chronograph 5980, establishing a new classic. Since its launch, various iterations of the 5960 have emerged, but we are excited to present an example in its original configuration: the platinum case model featuring a striking grey soleil dial. This design gives the watch a captivating depth, enhanced by elegant details such as leaf hands, luminous dots, and a sporty monocounter at the 6 o’clock position, complemented by a day/night indicator, power reserve, and an American-style calendar layout. This particular piece dates back to 2010 and comes as a full set being accompanied by its original sale papers, booklets, inner and outer box, delivery box with seal and accessories, including the white gold folding clasp, strap and corrector pin. The watch remains in excellent condition, having been preserved in great condition and seen minimal wrist time. Due to its collectibility and pristine state, having barely been worn, we have decided not to service or open the case yet, but on request of the client will be executed. We see this watch as an undervalued gem, overlooked yet poised to become a future classic due to its outstanding design and historical significance. If this remarkable timepiece resonates with you, we invite you to reach out to our private sales team, to discuss this exceptional Patek Philippe. Specifications: Patek Philippe 5960P Annual Calendar Flyback Chronograph Platinum case in 40.5mm 350xxxx / 447xxxx - 2010 Grey metallic soleil dial with annual calendar chrono display Inner/outer boxes, leather paper holder, booklets, and certificate of origin Patek Philippe’s CH 28-520 IRM QA 24h Calibre, first in-house automatic chronograph movement. Patek Philippe leather strap with platinum folding clasp Patek Philippe delivery box, seal and corrector pin Fully checked by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

The solid Oyster bracelet is a reference 93150, with 580 end links and a clasp stamped S11. title: Rolex Datejust Silver Dial with Original Papers Ref. 1600
description: The Rolex Datejust debuted in 1945 to celebrate Rolex’s 40th anniversary, becoming the first automatic wristwatch to feature a date displayed in a window at 3 o’clock, changing automatically at midnight. Few watches are as truly timeless and versatile as the classic 36mm Datejust — a watch suitable for any occasion. Here we have exactly that, with a subtle and desirable twist: the rarer reference 1600, distinguished by its smooth bezel. This example features an excellent case, paired with a lovely folded Jubilee bracelet (ref. 6251H) with 55 end links, and a superb pie-pan-style silver dial with a beautiful soleil finish. The dial showcases warm egg-yolk-colored lume plots, exciting and uncommon hour markers, and crisp black printed text, all contributing depth and character. Furthermore, the watch is accompanied by its original large Chronometer certificate and the rarer Japanese warranty paper — an outstanding bonus for collectors. This fine Datejust has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready to be enjoyed for many years to come. Interested in adding this exceptional Datejust to your collection? title: Cartier Tank Américaine Automatique in 18 k yellow gold ref 1740
description: The Cartier Tank Américaine was launched in 1989 as a modern interpretation of the classic Tank, inspired by the curved 1921 Tank Cintrée. It features a larger, more elongated case with a more pronounced curve and brancards along the flanks than its predecessor, yet with a flatter caseback. Originally, it was produced exclusively in precious metals. The model was designed to capture a more contemporary and bolder aesthetic, incorporating modern features such as automatic movements and water resistance in its larger versions. This example is an 18k yellow gold ref. 1740, measuring 26.5 × 45 mm. It features the signature Cartier blue cabochon-set crown, a white dial with black painted Roman numerals, a railway-style minute track, a hidden Cartier signature at the VII marker, a date window at six, and blued steel hands. The watch is paired with a Bulang and Sons strap, and its automatic movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. A watch built like an American tank, yet effortlessly chic — ready to make it yours? title: Patek Philippe Calatrava 3440J Automatic in 18k Yellow Gold
description: The Calatrava is one of the most refined wristwatches—if not the most refined wristwatch—and when paired with a nice leather strap, vintage yellow-gold models such as this reference 3440J become wonderfully casual for everyday wear. Introduced in 1961, it is notable for its water-resistant, screw-down case back, an uncommon feature for the time. It is estimated that only around 50 examples of this reference were produced. The 35 mm 18k yellow-gold case is in nice condition and features a distinctive hexagonal screw-down case back and unusual lugs . While the case may appear understated when viewed from the front, the Calatrava truly comes alive in profile, thanks to the three distinct angles elegantly cut into the downward-turning lugs. The dial is silvered and has aged to a soft cream tone, with lovely raised enamel Patek Philippe lettering. Typical for this reference, it features three vertically aligned applied markers at 12 o’clock, a “dot-dot” minute track, and a running seconds subdial on the lower half. It is fitted with fine, non-lumed stick hands and paired with a lovely leather strap, which we think is a perfect match. This elegant Calatrava is powered by the automatic Caliber 27-460 and has been fully serviced by our watchmaker, the setting crown has been replaced at this service. Our love for these fine Calatravas runs deep—let’s get in touch to talk!! title: Cartier Tortue "1847” 150th Anniversary in 18k Yellow Gold
description: The Cartier Tortue may not be as widely recognised as the Cartier Tank or the Cartier Santos, yet it remains one of Cartier’s most enduring and distinctive designs. Introduced in 1912, the Tortue was only the third wristwatch design created by Cartier, following the Santos-Dumont andthe Tonneau. Much like its early counterparts, it stood apart from the predominantly round watches of the era with its elegant tortoise shell–inspired shape. Despite its long history, the Tortue has always been produced in relatively small numbers, making it a rare and refined sight today. The present example is a lovely 18k yellow gold execution measuring 24 x 25 mm — a beautifully balanced and elegant proportion on the wrist. This particular version was created to celebrate the 150th anniversary of Cartier, founded in 1847. The dial is a crisp, bright white and features unusual red numerals along the minute track — a detail not often seen from Cartier. It retains the classic black painted Roman numerals, blued steel hands, and a subtle monogram pattern across the centre of the dial. At six o’clock, the dial is signed with “L❤️C,” which we interpret as “Love Cartier,” a charming and celebratory touch. The watch is paired with the strap shown and fitted with an original 18k yellow gold Cartier pin buckle. Its quartz movement has been fully checked by our watchmaker and fitted with a fresh battery, ready to be worn and enjoyed. A rare and characterful Tortue, combining historic design with a playful anniversary twist. Feel free to get in touch for further details. title: Patek Philippe Calatrava 5107R in 18k Rose Gołd Box and Paper Set
description: The Patek Philippe Calatrava was introduced in 1932, with the very first model being the Ref. 96. It took its name from the Order of Calatrava, whose cross would become one of the brand’s most recognisable emblems. The watch was developed under the leadership of Henri Stern, shortly after his family acquired Patek Philippe during the Great Depression. The Calatrava proved instrumental in guiding the company successfully through that challenging period — something we can confidently say it achieved. At hand we have the Patek Philippe Calatrava Ref. 5107R. Introduced at Baselworld in 2000, this reference marked an important step in bringing the Calatrava line into the 21st century, before later being replaced by the Ref. 5127. While preserving the timeless, Bauhaus-influenced design philosophy of the original Ref. 96, the 5107R introduced a slightly more sleek and sporty presence. Most notably, the addition of subtle crown guards both protects the crown and visually enhances the proportions of the 37mm case. This rose gold example also features a sapphire display case back, revealing the beautifully finished automatic Caliber 315 SC. The dial is presented in a warm opaline tone and features a dotted applied minute track, elegant boat-shaped applied hour markers, a date aperture at 3 o’clock, and sharp, refined hands that complete the balanced aesthetic. The watch is fitted with a lightly used Patek Philippe leather strap and its original 18k rose gold Patek Philippe pin buckle. Furthermore this marvel is completed with its original inner and outer boxes, the leather paper holder, booklets and its original certificate of origin stating a sale in 2002. Fully checked and regulated by our watch maker this rose gold marvel is ready for upcoming years. Experience the refined elegance of the Calatrava 5107—where classic dress watch sophistication meets a subtly sportier, modern edge ! title: Patek Philippe Ellipse 3738G Grey Soleil Dial Jumbo Automatic in 18k White Gold
description: The Patek Philippe Ellipse is a truly cool and distinctive watch—one we have admired for quite some time. Launched in 1968, the Ellipse was designed using the “divine proportion,” also known as the “golden ratio,” a mathematical formula celebrated for its harmonious and aesthetically pleasing balance, famously found in masterpieces such as Leonardo da Vinci’s Mona Lisa and the Great Pyramid of Giza. This principle, evident in art, design, architecture, and even in nature and the human body, underscores the Ellipse’s unique shape—neither round nor square, but perfectly balanced. This 18k white gold reference 3738 remains in fantastic condition and features a beautiful grey soleil dial, free of lume, fitted with simple stick-style hands. The watch is equipped with a lightly used original Patek Philippe leather strap and is fitted with a signature 18k Patek Philippe Ellipse pin buckle. The watch, along with its automatic calibre 240 movement, has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. Where design meets the golden ratio, this Ellipse awaits its next discerning owner, get in touch ! title: Rolex Oyster Perpetual Date Champagne Halliburton Logo Dial Box and Paper Set in Steel and Gold ref. 15223
description: Every so often, a watch surfaces that genuinely stops even seasoned collectors in their tracks. This is one of those pieces. A cool, well-executed steel and gold Rolex Oyster Perpetual Date, it features a crisp case, an 18k engine-turned bezel, and the highly uncommon Rolex Jubilee bracelet reference 62523H, complete with 474B-marked end links and an R8-stamped clasp. However, the true centerpiece of this watch is the champagne soleil dial bearing an exceptionally rare Halliburton corporate logo. Rolex has long produced special-order “logo dial” watches for royalty, military organizations, VIPs, and select corporations. This particular example was commissioned by the Houston-based multinational Halliburton, making it a fascinating and scarce piece of corporate Rolex history. The case back is engraved “Rainer Wrede HES 1998,” strongly suggesting a long-service or achievement award presented by Halliburton to an employee. Notably, this engraving is fully corroborated by the original paperwork. Collectors will especially appreciate that this watch is offered as a true full set. It includes both outer and inner Rolex boxes typical of the U.S. market, the correct U.S. cardboard paper holder, and the original guarantee paper showing a 1998 sale through authorized dealer Tourneau to Rainer Wrede—perfectly matching the case-back engraving. This rare and cool OP Date has also been fully serviced by our watchmaker. Rounding out the package are a red hangtag, a green hangtag bearing the watch’s serial number, three original Rolex booklets, and a Tourneau booklet. Logo dial, complete provenance, and a hint of Halliburton mischief—this OP Date is pure collector’s fun ! title: Lange & Söhne Saxonia Automatic in 18 k pink gold ref 380.033
description: It’s no secret that A. Lange & Söhne has been a top contender in haute horlogerie for years. Renowned for their attention to detail and pure elegance, they’ve become a serious competitor—so much so that some even wonder if they outshine brands like Patek Philippe. The Saxonia was one of the original four models introduced by Lange in 1994 and represents their take on the Calatrava style. This timeless timepiece exudes incredible elegance and meticulous craftsmanship. This particular example is crafted in 18k pink gold and is perfectly sized at 38 mm. title: Universal Genève “Cioccolatone“ 18k Rose Gold Oversized Dress-watch with unique Guilloché Dial on Brick Style Bracelet ref. 10228 2
description: Today, we have a unique offering: an 18k rose gold Universal Genève “Cioccolatone”!! The most defining feature is the distinctive curved square—or pillow-shaped—case, often described as a "curved square" or "quadrilateral." The name Cioccolatone is Italian for "big chocolate," referring to the case's resemblance to a square of Swiss chocolate. These oversized cases were mostly used from the 1940s until around 1960 by brands like Vacheron Constantin, Omega, Longines, and others. However, the design never really took off at the time—likely because square cases were more difficult to waterproof. Round watches could more easily accommodate screw-down case backs, whereas square watches couldn't. As a result, these designs were produced in limited quantities, making superb examples like this one incredibly rare today. In hand, we have a truly exceptional Universal Genève. Universal Genève (UG) was founded in 1894 as Universal Watch in Le Locle, Switzerland, by Numa Descombes and Ulysse Perret. The brand specialized in both watch movements and cases. After relocating to Geneva in 1919, UG gained prominence for its high-quality chronographs. and time only pieces like the iconic Compax and Polerouter models—and for its innovative micro-rotor automatic movements. The brand’s fortunes declined after the quartz crisis of the 1970s, but in 2023, the name was acquired by Breitling, signaling a potential revival and return to its legacy of excellence. This oversized 30 x 30 mm case (which wears even larger than the numbers suggest!) is crafted in 18k rose gold and features a striking sloped bezel—we’ve truly never seen another case like it. The snap-on case back retains both its serial and reference numbers, still fully readable and sharp. The second star of the show is the exquisite guilloché dial. Finished in a warm cream tone, it features two different guilloché techniques—almost giving it a sunray-like appearance. There’s no lume on the dial, and the sharp dauphine hands complete this elegant mid-century design perfectly. We’ve never seen another one like it—and perhaps we can even conclude this is a piece unique? The watch is paired with an 18k yellow gold brick-style bracelet with nine rows. While the bracelet is yellow gold (not rose), we love the look—it feels very period correct. It fits wrists up to 19 cm. Surprisingly, this is an automatic watch—powered by UG’s hammer automatic Caliber 138SS. This super-elegant dress watch has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. If we could keep a watch for ourselves, this would definitely be a contender, in hand it will blow one’s mind. A smart collector should snap this up—one would never regret owning such a beautiful piece! title: Cartier Vendôme Jumbo automatic “Paris” dial in 18 k yellow gold ref 17003
description: The year 1972 marked a turning point in Cartier’s history. A group of investors first acquired Cartier Paris, followed shortly by the acquisition of the New York and London branches. This consolidation reunited the three historically separate houses—divided after the death of Pierre Cartier—and laid the foundation for Cartier’s modern expansion. Another major milestone was Cartier’s partnership with Ebel, which led to relocating its watch production to La Chaux-de-Fonds, Switzerland. Until then, Cartier watches were primarily made in Paris (or occasionally London), using Swiss movements. Although some production continued in Paris during the 1970s, the collaboration with Ebel allowed Cartier to significantly scale up its output in Switzerland. This new chapter began in 1973 with the launch of Cartier’s first true historical reissue line: the Louis Cartier Collection. It started with 12 models that brought iconic designs from Cartier’s golden era—such as the Tank and Santos-Dumont—into serial production, while also introducing entirely new creations like the Cristallor, Fabergé, Gondole, and this example: the Vendôme. This particular example is the largest size at 33mm ( often referred to as Jumbo ”)and features automatic winding. The rounded case has no visible lugs; instead, it has a cutout within the case to accommodate the strap. It also features Cartier’s signature cabochon-topped crown. The classic white dial is signed “Paris. Additional details include a black “train track” minute scale and, of course, the signature black-painted Roman numerals. The blued hands add an elegant touch, and no seconds hand is present, staying true to the minimalist design. This refined Cartier is fitted with a gently worn Cartier leather strap, complete with its original 18k gold Cartier folding clasp and has been fully checked and regulated by our watchmaker. We have a passion for unusual Cartier watches and always aim to keep a diverse selection in stock for fellow Cartier enthusiasts. Let us know what you're looking for! Specifications: Cartier Vendôme Jumbo Reference 17003 18 k Yellow gold case in 33 mm Case number 1700312xx dates to the early 1970s White dial with Roman numerals “Paris” Blue cabochon winding crown Automatic Cal. 2670 movement Pictured Cartier leather strap, slightly used with a 18 k gold Cartier folding clasp Fully checked by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

Designed by Louis Cartier in 1917, the Tank has become an enduring icon of elegance and style. This example is a classic and elegant Ultra Thin Cartier Tank Louis, showcasing all the hallmarks of the brand—including a slightly cream-colored dial with painted Roman numerals and a railroad minute track. The watch features blued hands and a blue cabochon-topped winding crown. Both the dial and case are in lovely condition, with the dial also featuring a discreet Cartier signature at 7 o’clock and is signed SWISS at the 6 o'clock side. While the common Ref. 78086 typically uses the basic ETA-based Caliber 78-1, the 96065 and 96019 references—like this one—are more coveted for housing the ultra-thin, Cartier-branded Piguet 21 movement. This refined movement gives the Tank its “Ultra Thin” or Extra-Plate nickname, making it a top choice for collectors seeking a slimmer, more elegant profile. The watch is fitted with a fine black leather strap and an original 18k Cartier pin buckle. It has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is running beautifully. title: Explorer II 1655 Mk2 Frog Foot Dial “Albino”
description: The Rolex Explorer II reference 1655 holds a special place in the hearts of vintage Rolex collectors. First introduced in 1971, it was designed as a true tool watch, specifically for spelunkers and cave explorers with a fixed 24-hour bezel and a bold, highly legible layout. Over the years, subtle design variations in the dial and bezel have turned each iteration into a collectible in its own right. Unlike many of Rolex’s sport models that gained status-symbol recognition early on, the 1655 flew under the radar for quite some time. It was purely functional at launch, never intended to be flashy—which, ironically, makes it even more sought-after today. What we have here is a standout example of one of the earliest executions: a Mark 2, nicknamed the "Frog Foot" dial, as the coronet resembles a frog’s footprint. It also features the rare straight seconds hand, used before Rolex introduced the luminous "lollipop" tip in later versions—and a beautifully preserved steel bezel with centred thick numerals, giving the watch its distinctive character. Both the case and bezel are in strong, well-preserved condition, and the dial is beautiful, showcasing creamy yellow lume plots with a wonderfully aged tone. The unmistakable orange 24-hour hand has faded over time, taking on a pale yellow hue—a look affectionately nicknamed “Albino.” The watch is fitted with a folded Oyster bracelet, reference 7836, with 280 end links and a clasp stamped “1 71.” Included in the sale is an older Rolex service card from 2009. Fully serviced by our watchmaker, this Explorer II is ready for everyday wear—or to be tucked into a serious collector’s vault. Among matte-dial vintage Rolex sports models, the 1655 remains one of the coolest—and arguably most underappreciated. This example ticks all the right boxes: a bold faded-orange hand, clean straight seconds, and a warm, even yellow patina. Drop us a message if you're ready to explore this classic “Albino” for yourself—this one’s a gem! title: Omega SeaMaster 300 no date ref 165024
description: At the end of the 1940s, the first Omega Seamaster debuted, blending the elegance of a dress watch with the rugged durability required by military timepieces from WWII. Its innovative O-ring gasket provided superior water resistance, setting the stage for a long lineage of dependable sport watches. As recreational diving gained popularity, Omega began developing more specialized underwater tools. In 1957, the Seamaster 300 was introduced as Omega’s first true professional dive watch, featuring a unidirectional rotating bezel, luminous markers, and a genuinely water-resistant case—hallmarks that helped define the modern dive watch. As we know you guys love a watch built for adventure—and it’s getting harder to find these pieces in such great condition—the Omega Seamaster 300 remains one of the coolest tool watches out there and still offers strong value in today’s market. The dial has developed a beautiful patina, with warm yellow-toned hour markers. The hands are in good condition and match the markers nicely. We believe the hands are slightly later replacements; a 1963 example like this would originally have been born with baton hands. Nevertheless, the overall aesthetic is spot on. The asymmetrical case is strong and well-preserved all around. Inside beats the automatic, red-gilded Omega Caliber 552, which has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. We’ve paired this Seamaster with our Bulang & Sons rubber Nautic strap—a combination we think looks especially sharp. Love what you see? Let’s get this classic Seamaster on your wrist ! title: Rolex Oyster Perpetual Date 1500 Taupe Singer Proto/Test Dial in 14k Yellow Gold
description: We always try to offer you something uncommon or seldom seen — and today we have something special to propose once again. At hand is an unusual 14 kt yellow gold Oyster Perpetual Date, made for the British market. Eighteen-karat gold watches were much more expensive to import into certain countries, such as the USA and the UK. For that reason, many brands also offered 14 kt gold models. The most famous examples are the Rolex hand-wound Daytona models, where a 14 kt example can have a huge price difference compared to an 18 kt gold version nowadays — sometimes reaching differences of several hundred thousand! The case of this reference 1500 model, with its smooth bezel, is in excellent condition. Rolex decided to engrave the full serial number of this example inside the case back — a very uncommon feature. It is known that Rolex only did this for special deliveries, such as the famed military Submariners, the first “experimental” Sea-Dwellers (given to professional deep-sea divers for testing), unique pieces, and watches made for important collectors like the late Sultan Qaboos bin Said Al Said, who ordered a number of watches featuring the “Khanjar” coat of arms. The soleil-finished grey non lumed dial is another extremely uncommon feature. In fact, we have never seen another exactly like this. The dial resembles features like these unusual markers, very close in style to those made by the famous dial maker Singer, who produced the dials for what is arguably one of the most collectible watches of all time — the Daytona Exotic Dials. As the story goes, around a decade ago, a number of books containing loose dials were discovered — “study” dials from Singer, created as proposals to various brands including Universal Genève, Omega, and, as mentioned, Rolex. Some of these proposals were eventually used by Rolex in models such as the Daytona as well as in Datejust and other dressier models from that era. If you search “Rolex Singer prototype dials” online, a whole world opens up that helps illustrate what we are referring to. We have also previously had some rarer Datejusts with this feature and shared a small write-up on those as well over HERE We believe this dial was more a proto as “test for the market, meant as a way of testing the waters. It is extremely close to the more Art Deco–esque proposals by Singer, and perhaps this is why Rolex marked the watch with its full serial number inside the case back. To fully appreciate the dial, the decision was made to fit the watch with a non-loupe crystal, allowing a full and uninterrupted view of this rare variant. The watch has been paired with a beautiful Bulang and Sons leather strap, and both the watch and its movement have been fully serviced by our watchmaker. This is a rare opportunity to add a uniquely configured Rolex dress watch to your collection — and a true conversation starter. Let’s talk. Specifications: Rolex Reference 1500 Oyster Perpetual Date 14kt yellow gold 34mm case , smooth bezel ,

title: Breguet Ultra thin Classique Dress watch Guilloche dial ref 5130 in 18 k yellow gold
description: Abraham-Louis Breguet (1747–1823) established the Breguet brand in Paris, serving royalty and gaining renown for innovations such as the tourbillon (1801). He is widely considered one of the most important and influential figures in the history of watchmaking. This time-only reference 5130 is a quintessential dress watch, embodying timeless elegance. It features an ultra-thin 35.5 mm coin edge case with simple straight lugs and a discreet, small winding crown. The Ref. 5130 was part of the Breguet Classique collection, a line created to embody the aesthetic and technical principles established by Abraham-Louis Breguet in the late 18th and early 19th centuries. It maintains traditional Breguet design elements, including the signature Breguet hands with hollow moon tips and a hand-engraved, silvered gold guilloché dial. The dial features a silvered brushed outer chapter with fine Roman numerals, the Breguet signature at twelve o’clock, and a beautifully hand-engraved guilloché centre. Completing the look is the characteristic set of blued Breguet hands. This ultra-thin watch is powered by the automatic calibre 502, derived from the Frédéric Piguet calibre 71 (P-71), and has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. The watch is paired with one of our evergreen Bulang and Sons straps and fitted with an original 18k gold Breguet pin buckle. Available now for those who value timeless design and traditional watchmaking at its finest , get back to us ! Known for its minimalist design, the 1002 represents a classic and versatile era of Rolex history. title: Rolex Air-King 5500 Silver ADWOC Logo Dial
description: The Rolex Air-King released in the midst of World War II, the Rolex Air-King is the only model still in production from the original “Air Series.” Introduced in 1945, the Air-King joined the ranks of the Air-Lion, Air-Giant, and Air-Tiger, all created to honor RAF pilots who fought in the Battle of Britain. Like many Rolex models, the Air-King was designed with a particular type of wearer in mind—in this case, pilots. Here we have an early 1980s version, reference 5500, housed in a classic and sturdy 34 mm steel case. The case and its smooth bezel are in great overall condition, but the standout feature is the very rare logo dial. Rolex has long produced special-order “logo dial” watches for royalty, military organizations, VIPs, and select corporations. This particular example was commissioned by the Libyan company Arab Drilling & Workover Company (ADWOC). We believe the watch was gifted to an employee to mark years of service or another significant milestone in their career. The ADWOC logo is beautifully printed in red, with a drilling tower behind the lettering, set against a silver soleil-finished backdrop with crisp black printed text and the classic Air-King font. The watch is paired with an Oyster-style bracelet, ref. 78350, with end links marked 557 and a clasp stamped H.Further more the watch is accompanied with its original hang-tag bearing the serial of the watch. The watch and its automatic movement have been fully serviced by our watchmaker and are ready for many more years of use. Please get in touch to arrange a viewing or call and explore this rare and historic logo -Air-King in person! title: Rolex Turn- o-graph Silver linen dial ref 1625
description: For those newer to Rolex, the name Turn-O-Graph might not immediately ring a bell. Discontinued in 2011, it no longer appears in the Rolex catalogue and was often overshadowed by models like the Daytona, GMT-Master, and Submariner. Yet the Turn-O-Graph is a watch with a fascinating and significant history. Introduced in 1953, it was Rolex’s first commercially available watch with a rotating bezel—a feature often mistakenly credited to the Submariner—and the first sports model offered in Rolesor, the combination of steel and gold. By blending practical functionality with sporty elegance, the Turn-O-Graph laid the groundwork for what we now recognize as the modern tool watch. Its rotating bezel would go on to inspire defining features of some of Rolex’s most iconic sports watches, including the Submariner, and GMT-Master. Eventually, the Turn-O-Graph became integrated into the Datejust line, serving as its sporty variant. The example we have here is a classic 36 mm steel variant, featuring a turnable bezel in excellent condition and a beautifully preserved case. The stepped, pie-pan style silver dial is highlighted by striking black-topped hour markers, creating a sharp contrast and timeless appeal. It comes on a folded Jubilee bracelet, reference 6251H, with a clasp stamped “S W1” and end links marked 55. This sporty Datejust, complete with its slow-setting movement, has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready to wear. A watch like this, with its silver Linen dial, turnable bezel, and sporty charm, doesn’t come around often. Contact us today to arrange a viewing or to find out more! title: Cartier Gondole Ellipse Paris dial ref 67081
description: The Cartier Gondole Ellipse was launched in 1973 as part of the prestigious Louis Cartier Collection. This initiative by Cartier Paris aimed to revive legendary designs such as the Tank and Santos, while also introducing bold new geometric forms into serial production. Often grouped within the broader “Gondole” family, the Ellipse is a horizontal variation of the design. While the standard Gondole typically featured a more rectangular or “TV-shaped” case, the Ellipse introduced a squared-off oval form with subtly swollen sides, contrasting with the flat top and bottom lines characteristic of the Cartier Tank. This lovely reference 67081 is housed in an 18 kt yellow gold case measuring 26 × 23 mm, featuring a stepped bezel and a winding crown set with the signature Cartier cabochon. The dial displays the highly sought-after “Paris” signature at 6 o’clock—indicating it was sold through the legendary Place Vendôme boutique—and is classically finished with black Roman numerals and blued hands. The watch is fitted with a leather strap and an original 18 kt yellow gold Cartier folding clasp. The manual-winding movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready to be enjoyed for many years to come. No, it’s not a Tank. But yes—it absolutely deserves your attention. Get in touch. title: Longines Roulette Pocket Watch in 14k Yellow Gold
description: Every now and then, we come across a piece that isn’t a wristwatch yet still gives us that irresistible itch to buy it—simply because it makes us smile. What you’re looking at is a 14k yellow gold pocket watch made for the U.S. market, designed to resemble a miniature roulette table. The 41 mm circular case features an oversized bezel encircled by a freely rotating red-and-black enamel ring numbered 0 to 36, just like a real roulette wheel. Flip it over, and you’ll find a second enamel ring on the reverse, numbered 1 to 9. At the center sits a charming cream-silver dial with an outer minute track, applied yellow gold indices, Arabic numerals at 12, 3, and 9 o’clock, and a small seconds sub dial at 6 o’clock—signed “Longines” beneath 12. This delightful piece is powered by Longines’ hand-wound caliber 10L, fully checked and regulated by our watchmaker. Just look at it! Whether you keep it on your desk as a stress-relieving fidget spinner or carry it as a little slice of Las Vegas in your pocket, one thing’s for sure: the roulette wheel always spins! title: Audemars Piguet Disco Volante Ultra-thin Dress Watch in 18k Yellow Gold Champagne Dial
description: Although Audemars Piguet is best known for the Royal Oak, the brand’s legacy of exceptional timepieces extends far beyond. Among its most remarkable achievements are ultra-thin watches, a tradition dating back to 1938 with the legendary Caliber 9ML, measuring just 1.64mm thick. AP had already established itself as a master of slim movements, and this expertise laid the foundation for some of the most elegant and collectible dress watches of the mid-20th century. We are happy to offer an extraordinary example: a 1950s “Disco Volante,” or flying saucer, designed by German designer Gebhard Duve. The design was presented to Jacques-Louis Audemars, grandson of one of AP’s founders, and refined into a masterpiece of mid-century elegance. While other brands experimented with saucer-shaped cases, Duve’s interpretation is distinctive, combining Art Deco roots with the sleek minimalism of the 1950s. This 18k yellow gold version features short, elegant lugs, a crown seamlessly integrated into the case, and a brushed bezel. The champagne-colored dial is pure sophistication, with simple stick markers, stick hands without lume, and a small running seconds sub-dial at 6 o’clock. Powered by the legendary Audemars Piguet Caliber K2001—a movement also prized by Jaeger-LeCoultre and Vacheron Constantin—this Disco Volante is as technically impressive as it has a lovely lay out . Fully serviced by our watchmaker and paired with a Bulang & Sons leather strap, this ultra-thin piece wears beautifully on the wrist. Don’t miss the opportunity to own one of Audemars Piguet’s most refined and iconic mid-century creations—reach out today to make it yours.
