title: Longines 13ZN Chronograph Sandwich Dial in 18k Yellow Gold
description: In the mid-1930s, Longines created the legendary 13ZN — a movement that would become one of the most celebrated chronograph calibers in watchmaking history. Widely considered by vintage connoisseurs to be one of the finest and most sophisticated in-house chronograph movements of its era, the 13ZN remains a benchmark of technical excellence and craftsmanship. This example is fitted with an exceptionally rare “sandwich” dial, featuring cut-out numerals that reveal a second layer beneath, giving the dial its distinctive depth. It was an unusual and innovative design for its time — and even today it stands out. Only very few brands, such as Panerai (then produced by Rolex), experimented with a similar layered effect during that period. The dial is further equipped with a Base 1000 outer scale, a telemeter inner scale, a beautifully scripted “antimagnétique” inscription on the lower half, and two sub-registers for the chronograph. All of this is housed in an 18k yellow gold snap-back case fitted with the squared pushers typical of the era. The watch comes on the photographed leather strap and features an exceptionally rare 18k yellow gold pin buckle — a true gem in its own right. Both the case and the movement carry matching numbers, exactly as they should on authentic examples of this reference. The iconic yellow-gilded 13ZN movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is running beautifully. title: Tudor Date-Day 94510 Grey Dial
description: Debuted as the Oyster Prince Date+Day (Ref. 7017/0), the model coincided with Tudor’s logo change from the rose to the shield. While the Rolex version was produced exclusively in precious metals and measured 36 mm, Tudor’s counterpart was crafted in stainless steel and significantly larger at 38 mm, earning it the “Jumbo” nickname. Tudor later expanded the line with new references such as the 7019, featuring a rotating bezel, and the 7020. Subsequent models, including the 94510 and 94613, introduced more refined 36 mm cases that more closely mirrored the Rolex aesthetic, while retaining third-party ETA movements to keep prices accessible. At hand, we have a reference 94510 featuring a cool machined steel bezel on a robust 36 mm case. The dial is a seldom-seen grey colour, accented by stick hour markers and white printed text. The day disc, visible through the cut-out above the iconic shield logo, is in English. This fine Tudor is paired with a lovely folded Rolex-signed Oyster bracelet, reference 7836, with end links marked 558 and a clasp stamped “2/70.” The watch has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready for its next adventures. These Tudor dress watches remain somewhat underrated and under the radar, especially when compared to the brand’s more rugged Submariner and dive-watch offerings under the Shield. A sleeper classic, if you ask us— let’s discuss. title: Rolex Explorer 5504 Gilt Chapter-Ring Tropical Underline Dial
description: This reference 5504 was introduced in 1958, bridging the gap between a dress watch and a sports watch. It served as a more budget-friendly alternative to the pricier Explorer 6610, as the 6610 was chronometer-certified, whereas the 5504 used the new calibre 1530 movement—more compact yet still highly accurate. Because the movement was not chronometer-certified, the dial is marked “Super Precision” instead of “Chronometer.” The 5504 shared its 36 mm case and dial layout with the Air-King reference 5500, with the key differences being the larger case size (36 mm vs. 34 mm) and the Explorer-style dial. At hand we have a particularly attractive example of the 5504. Just look at that milk-chocolate-toned tropical dial! It features a super-glossy gilt dial with a chapter ring, the iconic 3-6-9 Explorer layout, silver-printed “Super Precision,” a “Swiss Only” marking at 6 o’clock, and a silver-printed underline right beneath the “Explorer” text. The underline—a small horizontal stroke—was used by Rolex as a transitional marker between roughly 1962 and 1964. This underline indicated an internal shift to the newer, lower-radiation tritium lume, distinguishing these dials from the earlier, highly radioactive radium versions. Produced for only a short period, underline dials are rare and highly sought after by collectors. This chocolate-gloss beauty is paired with a Rolex riveted Oyster bracelet, reference 7206, with 58 end links and a clasp stamped 1/64. The watch has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready for many years of wear. We believe this reference is something of an underdog—or simply under appreciated. A 36 mm Explorer-style watch with a gilt dial, tropical ageing, and an underline? Come on, guys—let’s get this wrapped around your wrist! Specifications: Rolex Explorer Reference 5504 Super precision 36mm steel case with smooth bezel Serial 949xxx dating to 1963 Tropical ,brown gilt chapter ring dial “Swiss only “with underline Riveted Oyster bracelet ref 7206 with end links 58 Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Rolex Explorer 14270 "Blackout Dial" Silver Print
description: The Rolex Explorer 14270 occupies a unique position in horological history. Caught in a sort of wristwatch purgatory—not old enough to be considered vintage and not new enough to be trendy—it is an underappreciated timepiece in collecting circles, yet it boasts a dedicated cult following among enthusiasts. The 14270 was introduced at the Basel Watch Fair in 1989, placing Rolex at the intersection of sport and luxury. The classic iteration of the tool watch was gradually being phased out as Rolex began integrating various higher-end manufacturing techniques into their timepieces. This period also saw other iconic tool watches undergoing significant design changes; for example, the Daytona received an automatic movement, while the Submariner was updated with sapphire glass and white gold surround indexes. The Explorer 14270 is so elegantly simple in its execution that it often goes unnoticed—no date, no fluted shiny bezel, just a straightforward three-hander. Here, we have the rarest dial of this reference, only found in early E and X series (from about 1990 to 1991), where the bold Arabic numerals are filled with glossy black enamel instead of white enamel as seen in later series. There are even two different variants of these: white print and the slightly rarer “silver print,” where all text is printed in silver—very uncommon! The case of our black dial remains quite robust, featuring a lovely smooth bezel. The dial has developed a slight “frozen” effect, where the black gloss has turned into a matte-like anthracite color, and the indexes have taken on a beige hue. We love it! The bracelet is in lovely condition, a ref 78360 solid Oyster type, with endlinks marked 558b and a clasp stamped R12. The watch has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. The Rolex Explorer 14270 bridges the gap between a dress watch and a sports watch. Do not miss out on this rare sleeper watch; get in touch! Specifications: - Rolex Reference 14270 - 36mm steel case - Serial E89xxxx dating to 1990 - Rare ‘Blackout’ Dial with silver print - Oyster bracelet ref 78360 with 558b endlinks - Fully serviced by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: IWC Portofino Blue soleil dial box and paper set" Khanjar "ref IW356523
description: IWC was founded in 1868 by American watchmaker Florentine Ariosto Jones in Schaffhausen, Switzerland, with the goal of combining American mass-production techniques with Swiss craftsmanship to create high-quality pocket watches. The company, now known as IWC Schaffhausen, is a renowned watch manufacturer that has expanded into wristwatches and innovative materials while remaining true to its engineering-focused philosophy. The IWC Portofino collection evolved from a design inspired by a classic pocket watch and has since become one of IWC's most successful lines, known for its understated elegance and timeless aesthetics. The full Portofino collection was officially launched in 1984, named after the picturesque Italian fishing village to evoke the spirit of la dolce vita . At hand, we have a Portofino in a 39 mm steel case with a blue soleil-finished dial, a date function, and rose-gold hour markers paired with leaf-style hands. Turning the watch over, you’ll find a striking “Khanjar” engraved on the case back. A khanjar is a traditional Omani ceremonial dagger with a curved, J-shaped blade. Worn by men as a symbol of status, masculinity, and national pride, it forms part of the traditional Omani dress for formal, religious, and celebratory occasions. Often ornately decorated, the khanjar is also the national emblem of Oman. This watch was gifted to someone special by the late Sultan Qaboos bin Said al Said. In the watch-collecting world, it is well known that the Sultan was an avid admirer of fine timepieces, and quite a number of Rolex, Cartier, and IWC models bearing this emblem are documented. This Portofino comes on its original IWC leather strap, fitted with a steel IWC pin buckle. The watch includes its original inner and outer boxes, with the outer box once again featuring a red Khanjar printed on top. It also comes with its original guarantee card and a “thank you” card in Arabic. The watch has been fully checked and regulated by our watchmaker. Ready to make this Khanjar-engraved piece part of your story? Reach out and we’ll guide you the rest of the way. Specifications: IWC reference IW356523 Portofino 39 mm steel case Blue dial rose gold markers and hands , blue date Serial 707xxxx serial dates to the early 2010’s IWC Leather strap and steel IWC buckle Original IWC guarantee card Inner and outer boxes , outer box Khanjar emblem Fully checked by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Blancpain Fifty fathoms Rotomatic 1950's
description: The Blancpain Fifty Fathoms holds a legendary place in dive watch history. The name Fifty Fathoms might sound unusual at first, but it refers to a British unit of measurement. One fathom equals roughly six feet, so fifty fathoms translates to about 91 meters—or close to 300 feet—the maximum depth divers could reach with the technology of the era. Over the decades, Fifty Fathoms watches have earned serious credibility through military use, trusted by elite forces from France, Germany, Poland , Finland, and most famously, the U.S. Navy. This particular 34mm example stands out not only for its compact size but also for its remarkable condition. Often, these dials turn matte or suffer from paint loss, but this one has held up beautifully. The watch features a fantastic case, a stunning black gloss dial with an applied Blancpain logo, and a beautifully scripted Fifty Fathoms signature. The hands are of the pencil type, and the seconds hand has a distinctive spear shape. The lume on the indexes appears to have been re-applied, but it still matches nicely in color with the handset. The Bakelite bezel is also in excellent condition. Powered by an automatic Rayville/Blancpain 1361 movement, this piece is a fantastic addition to any dive watch collection. It comes fitted on a rubber Nautic strap, reminiscent of how these watches were originally delivered. We’re on a roll with Fifty Fathoms models at the moment—we have some contemporary limited editions as well . We’ve even got some of its older brothers-in-arms, including original military issued models .. Feel free to reach out—we’ll be happy to show you what’s available! title: Rolex Submariner 6205 Small Crown "First Submariner"
description: The 1950s proved to be a defining era for Rolex, highlighted by the introduction of some of its most legendary models: the Submariner, GMT-Master, Day-Date, and Explorer lines. As scuba diving gained traction among adventurers and professionals alike, Rolex recognized the growing demand for a dependable dive watch to help monitor time spent underwater and reduce the risk of decompression sickness, also known as “the bends.” There was also a need for a reliable timing device to track the remaining “breathing time.” This vision culminated in the creation of the Submariner—a timepiece that would come to represent not only technical excellence and rugged reliability but also timeless design. The Rolex Submariner reference 6205 was introduced in 1954 as one of the earliest Submariner models, alongside references 6204 and 6200. The 6205 was produced for only about a year, making it a rare and highly sought-after collector's item. As Rolex themselves put it in the book Oyster Perpetual Submariner: The Watch That Unlocked the Deep , just 810 examples were produced. To put that in perspective: approximately 2,800 reference 6538 "Big Crown" Submariners were made. A signficant innovation of the 6205 was the introduction of the "Submariner" name on the dial, a feature that has remained on all subsequent Submariner models, so maybe we can conclude this is the “real “first Submariner! The “ no depth rating “ dial is still in nice condition, with the original pencil and “lollipop” seconds hands present. Although the hands have been re-lumed in the pas they still look charming. The 37.5 mm “small crown “ case stays in nice condition .The so-called “no-hash” bezel insert remains on the watch in unmolested condition, though we believe the bezel itself is a later replacement and has been paired with one of our leather straps. This super early “Neanderthaler” Sub has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready to join a Submariner-themed collection. We've enjoyed this watch for some time, and it has truly grown on us. Get in touch. Specifications: Rolex Reference 6205 Submariner 37.5 mm steel case “small crown - no crown-guards” “No -hash“ bezel insert Serial 988xxx dating to 1955 Swiss only dial , “Submariner“ no depth Pencil hands , with “lollipop“ second hand - relumed Coming on the photographed Bulang and sons strap Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Rolex Submariner Gilt dial ref 5513 first owner provenance
description: We always love discovering watches with a personal history—and this one comes from a passionate diver in Germany! This exceptional 5513 features a strong case paired with a beautifully faded dark grey insert that shows subtle hints of brown. It’s matched with a mirror-like gilt dial and warm yellow patina on the lume of the hour markers and hands. Absolutely stunning. These early black-dial Rolex watches feature what collectors refer to as "gilt dials." The text and outer minute track are printed in a gold tone—hence the term "gilt." In fact, the gold color is the brass dial base showing through, as the dials were printed with the text in relief. The case remains strong all around, with well-defined, even lugs and crown guards. In direct light, a touch of tropical brown emerges from the dial, adding to its vintage charm. The case back is engraved with "Günter Greie Kiel," the name of the original owner of this incredible Submariner. There’s also a fascinating link to this engraving: included in the sale is a photocopy of Günter’s diving pass from the 1960s. Born in the 1930s, Günter joined the "Diving Group Kiel, Germany" in 1964—one of the oldest diving clubs in the country. Notably, many members of the Federal Marine Corps were part of this club, and Kiel remains the home of the German Naval Defense Force headquarters. The watch is fitted with a Rolex reference 7205 riveted bracelet, with end links marked 64 and a clasp stamped 74. It has been fully serviced by our expert watchmaker and is running smoothly. The 5513 is truly the cornerstone of the iconic no-date Rolex Submariner line—and one of our all-time favorites here at Bulang and Sons, which is why we proudly founded the No Date Sub Club. Feel free to reach out if you’d like to discuss this remarkable piece further! title: Tudor Prince Date "Small block" Chronograph Black Dial ref. 79260
description: In 1995, Tudor introduced the Prince Chronograph, marking a significant shift in the design of its chronograph line. Prior models, including the well-known Big Block series, were characterized by their chunky cases, flat sides, and bold wrist presence. With the launch of the Prince Chronograph, Tudor moved in a new direction — the case was completely reworked with softer contours and a more refined silhouette, closely echoing the design language of the Rolex Daytona. This updated look was complemented by the addition of a sapphire crystal, which gave the watch a sleeker profile and a more modern feel on the wrist. This Ref. 79260 features a lovely case, an unmolested black "Units per Hour" fixed tachymeter bezel, and a stunning gloss black dial with three silver gloss sub-dials. The “Jubilee”-style five-row link bracelet is a Ref. 62490, with solid end links and a clasp marked “AB4.” This cool Small Block has been fully checked by our watchmaker, and its Tudor-modified Valjoux 7750 automatic movement has been regulated. Often considered something of a ‘sleeping giant’, these fourth-series chronographs are finally gaining traction as collectors begin to appreciate their modern aesthetics and exceptional build quality. Get in touch! title: Heuer Skipper Yacht Regatta Timer Blue dial ref. 73463 with rare Gay Frères bracelet
description: The Heuer Skipper was introduced in 1968, specifically designed for yacht racing and regattas. Unlike Heuer’s racing-focused chronographs like the Autavia or Carrera, the Skipper featured a distinctive countdown timer—crucial for timing the start sequence of sailboat races. The earliest Skippers (such as the rare 7754 “Skipperera”) were based on Carrera cases. However, by the 1970s, Heuer began using Autavia-style cases, blending nautical functionality with motorsport ruggedness. The watch we’re offering is reference 73463 — a quintessential product of the 1970s. Based on the Autavia 73663 case, it features a large, cushion-shaped 42mm steel case in excellent condition, complete with pump pushers and a rotating bezel that adds a rugged, sporty feel. The blue, slightly faded turnable hour bezel is also in great shape. The blue dial features two sub-dials: on the right, the iconic three-color countdown timer; on the left, a minute counter, with a date window positioned at 6 o’clock. This fine Skipper is paired with a rare and highly desirable Gay-Frères 9-row bracelet, featuring end links marked “HLF” and a clasp stamped “4 71.” This 1970s chronograph has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready for its next adventure.The original winding/setting crown has been replaced at service . Enquire now to add this iconic Heuer Skipper to your lineup. title: Patek Philippe Golden Ellipse 3546G Silver sigma dial in 18k white gold
description: The Patek Philippe Golden Ellipse broke the mold for dress watches when it debuted in the late 1960s. Its shape was revolutionary—but not for the sake of being different. Inspired by the timeless beauty of the golden ratio, it offered a refined balance between round and rectangular forms, setting it apart from traditional watchmaking norms. Rather than chasing trends, Patek carved its own path—creating a design that was both distinctive and enduring. More than 50 years later, the Golden Ellipse remains one of the brand’s most iconic collections, second only to the legendary Calatrava. Here we have something special: the rarely seen reference 3546. This version boldly steps outside the usual Golden Ellipse formula by introducing lugs to the case design—a quiet twist on a timeless classic. The white gold case measures 32 x 27 mm and features a snapback and a small winding crown adorned with the iconic Calatrava cross. This example is fitted with a unique silver dial that has an almost “hammerite” texture, complete with sigma markings, painted black Roman numerals, and elegant black stick hands. The watch is paired with a refined Bulang and Sons leather strap and comes with an original 18k white gold Patek Philippe pin buckle. Powering this understated piece is the hand-wound Caliber 23-300, which has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. We’ve always loved how the Ellipse line reflects Patek’s creative side—more than just watches, it became a full design language, extending into objects like lighters, desk clocks, and even jewelry. It’s a subtle statement, full of character. Looking for something a little off the beaten path? This one might be it—drop us a line to chat more. title: Heuer Autavia "Shauntavia" ref 741.603
description: What you're looking at is one of the rarest 1970s Autavias: the so-called “Shauntavia,” nicknamed after collector Shaun Wainstein, who conducted in-depth research on these models. This reference is often—if we can even use the word “often” for something this rare—seen with a Kenyan Air Force (KAF) marking, including a military issue number. These watches were issued to pilots and senior officers in the Air Force. This particular piece does not have the military marking, as it’s believed within the Heuer collector community that roughly half of these references were sold to the general public. The classic 1970s cushion-shaped 42mm case is in fine condition, featuring a bezel insert that has faded beautifully to a gunmetal grey. What makes this Autavia especially distinctive is its dial variant, which features bold, luminous Arabic numerals, two chronograph sub-dials, and a date window at 6 o’clock. Also noteworthy are the large white pencil-style hands with red tips, a red chronograph seconds hand, and black hands on the recessed sub-dials. Powering the watch is the hand-wound Caliber 7741, which has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. This striking tool watch comes on a Bulang and Sons racing-style strap, which we think pairs perfectly with its character. This is one of the rarest Heuer Autavia’s get in touch to get you this Shauntavia ! title: Blancpain Fifty Fathoms "No Rad" Date
description: The Fifty Fathoms was originally developed in collaboration with the French Navy’s elite combat divers—and its legacy lives on today, even in modern pop culture, thanks to the recent Blancpain × Swatch Bioceramic Scuba collection. But if you’ve got the Swatch, you really need to experience the original. This is a desirable 41mm example in beautiful, honest condition—exactly what we love to see. The name Fifty Fathoms might sound unusual, but it refers to a British unit of measurement. A fathom equals about six feet, so fifty fathoms translates to roughly 91 meters—close to 300 feet—the maximum depth divers could reach with the technology available at the time. Fifty Fathoms watches have seen serious military use over the decades, trusted by forces from France, Germany, Sweden, Norway, Denmark, Finland, and most famously, the U.S. Navy. This particular version is the highly sought-after "No Radiations" model, often nicknamed the “No-Rad.” What makes it special? That iconic yellow-and-red crossed-out radiation symbol on the dial. It signified that no radium was used in the luminous material—a move made in the early 1960s as the health risks of radium became more widely known. The “No Rad” logo offered peace of mind to both professional and recreational divers. Due to their historical significance—and that instantly recognizable symbol—these models are now highly prized by collectors. Blancpain has even released tribute editions of the Fifty Fathoms No Rad, cementing its place in the brand’s history. The matte case is in fine condition with a beautiful luminous rotating dive bezel. The dial remains glossy, featuring an applied Blancpain logo, a date window, warm yellow patina on the lume, and a handsome pencil-style handset with matching yellow lume. We’ve paired this iconic diver with one of our Nautic straps—similar to the original rubber straps these “fifties” were delivered with back in the day. The automatic movement (based on an A. Schild caliber AS.1700) has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. For collectors and enthusiasts, the "No Rad" is more than just a beautiful timepiece—it’s a symbol of a bygone era when watches were true tools built for survival. Let’s talk. title: Audemars Piguet Royal Oak Ref. 6001ST
description: The reference 6001 is a watch you’re unlikely to have encountered before. It’s not a Royal Oak, designed by Jean-Fred Meylan (not Gérald Genta), and visually, it shares little in common with any other Audemars Piguet model, aside from its small tapisserie dial. Fewer than 350 pieces are believed to have been produced between 1974 and 1978, after which it was replaced by the reference 6005, which bears a much closer resemblance to the Royal Oak (though it’s not called that!). The 6001 was a bold experiment, introduced during the height of the "quartz revolution." In the end, Audemars Piguet embraced quartz technology, using a modified Beta 21 movement from Omega, known as the 'megaquartz' for its remarkable 2,359,296 vibrations per second. This movement would lose or gain no more than one second per month. That small pusher near the crown? It’s used to fine-tune the time by correcting that single second. The "rounded yet octagonal" case has soft, flowing edges, far removed from the angular lines of the original Royal Oak. It has a very 1970s, almost Star Wars-esque vibe mixed with Royal Oak influences—a design that feels both futuristic and retro at the same time! The 35 x 40 mm steel case remains in excellent condition, and the integrated AP bracelet (which fits wrists up to 18 cm) is also in great shape. The signature petite tapisserie dial is in blue, featuring an applied AP logo on the lower half (just as the original Royal Oak 5402 would have had during its era!) with a date at 6 o’clock. The hands are typical of AP’s design, with the first series of Royal Oak tritium markers. The quartz-driven movement (caliber 2510) has been fully checked by our watchmaker and a fresh battery is fitted. This is a rare watch from one of the “Holy Trinity” of watch brands. Come on, guys—this is a conversation piece and super cool! Get in touch! title: Smiths "Deluxe" ref 6B/542 Military Watch for the British RAF
description: In the late 1940s and early 1950s, Smiths was commissioned to produce a General Service wristwatch for the British Armed Forces. The British government, concerned about dependence on foreign watch suppliers—something that had proven problematic during World War II—offered Smiths substantial contracts to encourage domestic watch production. However, for reasons unknown, the British Army ultimately chose not to move forward with the order. It wasn’t until the late 1960s that Smiths secured a military contract—this time for the W10 model. These watches were assigned the same 6B/542 stores reference as the Omega navigators’ watch, suggesting they were likely issued to RAF pilots and navigators. This is the rarest Smiths British military watch, and arguably one of the rarest British military watches of all. Approximately 300 of these watches were made and issued to the RAF in 1954, 1955, and 1956—presumably as a test order. This would make sense for a trial run, as the inside of an aircraft cockpit presented a challenging environment for mechanical wristwatches, mainly due to the presence of electromagnetic fields. To combat this, the watches were equipped with an iron dial and a metal dust cover, which together created an effective Faraday cage to shield the movement from electromagnetic interference. This particular Smiths was issued in 1955, as evidenced by its issue number on the case back. The case is in lovely condition, with fixed bars for fitting a single-pass strap—so military personnel had a slimmer chance of losing the watch during duties. This example has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready for new adventures in the years to come. A fun fact about these Smiths Deluxe watches: there were also some non-issued, non-military-marked versions. This particular model, housed in a water-resistant case, was worn by Sir Edmund Hillary during his ascent of Everest (and you thought it was all about Rolex!). The Smiths De Luxe A454 and A460 models, known as the ‘Antarctic’ versions, were supplied to the 1957 British Antarctic expedition, in which Hillary also participated. These models feature a silver-colored dial and a finer outer minute track. These super RARE purpose-built watches hold real history and have a super cool wrist presence, we think. Get in touch! Specifications: - Smiths Deluxe military wristwatch 6B/542 - 35mm steel case with Faraday inner case back, military inventory numbers on case back, fixed bars - Black dial with painted Arabic hours, King’s cross on dial, sword-style hands - Issue number 6B/542 12xxx /55 dating to 1955 - Hand-wound movement - Comes on the pictured single-pass strap - Fully serviced by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Tudor "Monte Carlo " chronograph grey dial ref 7149 / 0 “mystery  engraving “

title: Aquadive Time-Depth Electronic Dive Watch
description: Ah, the 1970s—a time when tool watches meant business. No compromises, no concern for slipping under a cuff—just pure function-driven design. This hefty 46mm dive watch is a true engineering marvel, featuring a battery-powered movement that winds the mainspring and utilizes a unique diaphragm system to measure and display depth underwater. At 12 o’clock, you’ll find a valve that allows water to enter a metal tube filled with oil inside the case—an incredibly innovative approach to depth measurement! For added functionality, the crown is positioned on the left-hand side, likely to prevent accidental unwinding during a dive. The unidirectional dive bezel sits atop the robust case, ensuring safe timing underwater. The dial is a striking black and white design, with a bold orange fourth hand to display real-time depth readings. And here’s a cool detail: the movement is signed by Tissot, adding an unexpected touch of horological intrigue. To complete its funky, chunky 1970s tool-watch aesthetic, we’ve paired it with a single-pass strap that perfectly matches its rugged spirit. If you love innovative, purpose-built dive watches, this Aquadive Time-Depth is calling your name. Get in touch! Specifications: - Aquadive Time-Depth dive watch - Water depth measurement, displayed via a fourth hand - 46mm stainless steel case with valve at 12 o’clock and left-side crown - Unidirectional rotating dive bezel - Tissot-signed movement, battery-powered - Single-pass strap - Fully checked by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: IWC Ingenieur SL Jumbo Quartz Black Dial Ref. 3303
description: The IWC Ingenieur was first introduced in 1954 as a tool watch specifically designed for engineers and technicians who needed a timepiece that could withstand magnetic fields and harsh environments. The original model featured a soft iron inner case to protect the movement from magnetic interference. Over the years, while the design has evolved, the Ingenieur has always remained true to its original purpose: a rugged, reliable timepiece built for professionals. In the mid-1970s, IWC enlisted the renowned designer Gerald Genta to update the Ingenieur’s design, resulting in a more modern, fashionable aesthetic. The “Genta” Ingenieur debuted in 1976 and hit stores in 1977, featuring a large case with an integrated bracelet and powered by the automatic c.8541ES movement. Due to its substantial size, the mechanical Ingenieur ref. 1832 quickly earned the affectionate nickname “Fat” Ingenieur. However, despite its innovative design, it struggled to gain market traction, mainly due to its high price and the onset of the "quartz crisis." To adapt, IWC released two quartz versions: the first with the quartz calibre 2405, followed by a slimmer model with the quartz calibre 2250, a more comfortable bracelet, and a refined case design. In 1980, the ref. 3303 replaced the ref. 3003, offering a flatter case and the same quartz calibre 2250. The present watch is a rare and highly sought-after IWC “Genta” Ingenieur reference 3303, featuring a quartz movement. With only 149 examples ever produced, and even fewer appearing on the market, this timepiece is truly a collector's gem! We’re especially fond of the stunning black "waffle dial," a signature design element from IWC that almost resembles carbon fiber. The iconic Genta-designed three-row bracelet is in excellent condition, fitting wrists up to 18.5 cm, and the signature “fish crown” remains intact. The watch has been fully checked by our watchmaker and is fitted with a fresh battery. Reach out to us to learn more about this cool, iconic IWC designed by Genta! Specifications: - IWC Ingenieur SL Reference 3303 - 40mm steel case - Serial 226xxx dating to the early 1980s - Black ‘waffle’ dial - Pencil hands - Integrated three-row steel bracelet - IWC calibre 2250 quartz movement - Fully checked by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Blancpain Fifty Fathoms 3H Bundeswehr
description: This is a rare and fascinating Blancpain Fifty Fathoms, crafted for the German Bundeswehr frogmen in the 1970s. These watches were not available for public sale but were exclusively issued! You'll notice that this watch departs from the typical Blancpain Fifty Fathoms appearance due to its unique bezel insert. Since the Bund divers used rebreathers and didn't require the graduated dive bezels seen on Rolex Subs, the insert is plain and numeral-free. It's uncommon to find one of these watches in such good and original condition, particularly with the bakelite bezel intact. This one has a crack at the tritium triangle at 12, a testament to its use as intended. The bakelite beneath the triangle is almost nonexistent, as this is a common stress point. The 41mm case is strong and features fixed strap bars, with a winding crown positioned at five o’clock. The case back displays the Bund engraving: 6645-12-171-41xx . The original hands contain tritium lume, showing a bit of “military life,” and they match well with the tritium hour markers, indicated by the 3H on the dial. We especially love the vibrant orange minute hand! It’s shown here on a single pass strap. The automatic movement has been thoroughly serviced by our watchmaker. Finding pieces like this in such condition is a rarity. This is indeed a special watch—please get in touch to experience this tool watch vibe on your wrist! Issued watches like this are true gems, and we’re thrilled about this find. Let’s chat and make a deal! Specifications: - Blancpain Fifty Fathoms - Issued to German forces (Bundeswehr) in the 1970s - Serial 12XX - Military case back engravings: 6645-12-171-41xx - Steel 41mm case - Black dial with 3H marking - Original blank bakelite bezel insert - Original funky hands with orange minute hand - Comes on the pictured Bulang and Sons single pass strap - Fully serviced by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Includes a handmade leather watch pouch - Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: IWC Da Vinci Perpetual Calendar Silver Dial Ref. IW375803
description: The IWC Da Vinci line emerged in 1969 with the introduction of the very first IWC Da Vinci, equipped with the infamous Beta 21 quartz movement. However, the watch we have at hand is a very different kind of timepiece! The first significant evolution of the collection occurred in 1985 with what is undoubtedly the most famous version: the Da Vinci Perpetual Calendar (Ref. 3750). This model was created under the technical guidance of Kurt Klaus, while Günter Blümlein served as CEO of IWC. The launch of this watch is quite significant, and the context warrants explanation. During the early 1980s, the Swiss watch industry faced massive changes. Interest in quality mechanical timepieces—ranging from simple three-hand watches to complex pieces—was limited to a small group of collectors and enthusiasts. Wearers of mechanical watches were rare and often perceived as “old-school” or “outdated.” While sales representatives from historic brands like IWC attempted to convey the beauty of mechanical movements and the dedication of watchmakers in crafting fine timepieces, clients were captivated by the slim and precise electronic quartz watches. At the Basel Watch Fair in 1985, a new watch was introduced that combined striking design with a complex perpetual calendar chronograph movement (the ancestor of the watch we have today): the Da Vinci Perpetual Calendar (Ref. 3750). This watch, the first in the new Da Vinci collection, is the result of designer Hano Burtscher’s vision, featuring a round design with articulated swivel lugs. More importantly, this watch symbolizes the resurgence of complex mechanical movements. At the end of the 1970s, Kurt Klaus developed a perpetual calendar module that allowed all settings to be made via the crown, easily attachable to almost any movement—from a simple three-hander to a chronograph. Our watch is based on this design language and movement, featuring a nicely sized 41mm steel case with “mushroom” pushers. There is a lot to take in when looking at the dial: it features a white background with painted Arabic numeral hour markers, a 12-hour chronograph, a perpetual calendar displaying the day, date, month, and four-digit year, as well as a moonphase! All of this is protected by a sapphire crystal. The moonphase has a lovely sparkle, a little detail we truly appreciate. All settings can be adjusted using the crown at 3 o’clock—a very ingenious design! This lovely IWC comes on a Bulang & Sons strap and has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. We believe this is a steal at this price—just look at what you're getting in this range. Let's get a great deal done! Specifications: - IWC Da Vinci Perpetual Calendar - Reference IW375803 - Dates to 2004–2006 - 41mm steel stepped case with swivel lugs and “mushroom” pushers - White/silver dial with day, date, month, and four-digit year display in English, with black Arabic numerals - Chronograph, perpetual calendar, and moonphase - Luminous hands - IWC Calibre C.79261 automatic moveme

title: Lemania Monopusher Chronograph 6BB "Asymmetric Case" Military Chronograph Black Dial
description: Lemania, established in 1884, is one of the most respected names in vintage chronographs. Known for their robust and reliable chronograph movements, Lemania has earned a lasting legacy, especially through their collaborations with Omega, Patek Philippe, and Breguet. Their military-issued watches, designed for extreme environments, exemplify the true essence of a tool watch. This particular Lemania monopusher chronograph is a remarkable example of military-issued timepieces. The 38mm asymmetric steel case features a crown guard on the right side, protecting the crown and pusher from damage. The case is in fantastic condition, and the case back bears military engravings, including "6BB-944-3306," signifying its initial issuance to the Royal Air Force. Later, it was used by sailors and submarine personnel in the Royal Navy. The black dial is non-lumed (a feature to avoid any radiation exposure in submarines), and it is adorned with orange lume for a vintage touch. The syringe hands and "circle T" for Tritium designation give it a distinctly military feel, while the dial also features the iconic "King's Cross" marking. The watch has a hand-wound Lemania Caliber 2220 movement, which has been fully serviced by our watchmaker for optimal performance. This military chronograph is paired with a single-pass strap, as per MOD regulations, and remains a fascinating and historically significant piece. Specifications: - Lemania Monopusher Chronograph "6BB" - 38mm steel asymmetric case with fixed lugs - Case No. 5xx / 70, dating to 1970 - Military engravings on caseback, issued to the GB armed forces - Black broad arrow dial with luminous Arabic numerals - Syringe hands - Lemania Caliber 2220 movement - Comes on a single-pass strap (per MOD rules) - Fully serviced by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Czapek Antarctique "Passage de Drake" Unique Silver Dial Full Set
description: Czapek is a young yet highly respected independent watchmaker with an innovative business model based on crowdfunding, boasting over 200 shareholders. The brand's unique horizontal integration treats suppliers as partners, ensuring exceptional quality and collaboration. Until 2020, Czapek's collection was a homage to classic luxury, inspired by the designs of Czech-born watchmaker François Czapek. These timepieces exuded elegance and sophistication, appealing to connoisseurs of traditional watchmaking. In a bold move that redefined its identity, Czapek introduced the Antarctique—a luxury sports watch designed to make waves in the competitive market of integrated bracelet timepieces. The integrated bracelet features a three-row design with an easy-release system, allowing for quick changes to the included white rubber strap (unworn and included in the package). The bracelet seamlessly follows the lines of the case, with C-shaped middle links paying homage to the brand’s name. The first standout feature of this watch is its stunning dial, named “Stairway to Eternity.” This unique trapezoid dial motif, with its three-dimensional stamped surface, creates a captivating play of shadows. The technique, called Flinqué, adds a delightful touch. The dial itself was on request and is unique, it is a combination of the Antarctique and Passage de Drake series. Czapek will not make another one of these, so they told. The sword-shaped minute and hour hands and the splash of red on the seconds hand add to its three-dimensionality. The second highlight is the Calibre SXH5, visible through the display caseback. It features a platinum micro-rotor and impeccable finishing, creating a striking contrast. The movement boasts a modern yet intricate design, with hand-finished chamfered inner angles and meticulously detailed flanks. The watch is in pristine 10/10 condition and comes with its Czapek outer box and a stunning handmade inner box that doubles as a workshop for changing straps and adjusting the bracelet. The package includes slots for screwdrivers (for bracelet adjustment), an unworn Czapek rubber strap, a booklet, and the “Czapek Passport” stating the case number and the name of the watchmaker who built the watch. These watches were made to order, limited to one watch per day in 2021. Czapek also gives the option for a custom engraving. This watch was engraved with the coordinates of Ernest Shackleton’s shipwreck, named ‘The Endurance’. Ernest Shackleton lead multiple Arctic expeditions, leaving behind astounding world famous stories. An Arctic Explorer given stage on the back of this Czapek Antarctique, the former owner had a feeling for the finer things.. The watch has been fully checked by our watchmaker and is still under the three-year warranty offered by Czapek. Discover the elegance and innovation of Czapek with the Antarctique "Passage de Drake," a watch that combines modern sophistication with timeless craftsmanship. Specifications: - Czapek Antarctiq

title: Boucheron Art Deco Pencil Watch
description: Founded in 1858 by Frédéric Boucheron, the House of Boucheron is a luxury retailer that specializes in beautifully crafted watches, jewelry, and accessories from its prestigious flagship location on Place Vendome in Paris. The brand's creations are celebrated for their elegance and innovation, including groundbreaking designs like the clasp-less necklace and the integration of diamonds with pearls. This is an interesting pencil in Chinese lacquer with a hidden watch in the top half. The pencil remains in excellent condition. The top section of the pencil houses a small watch with manual wind movement, with winding crown to the right. The black dial has gold printing in an Art Deco style. The watch can be concealed by rotating the barrel of the pencil. This cool object d’art comes in its original Boucheron box from Bond Street London. Our watchmaker has fully serviced the movement. This is a lovely horological gem for your desk. Get in touch and let’s draw up a sale! Specifications: - Boucheron Pencil Watch - Black Lacquer finish - Black dial with gold print - Gold hands - Manual wind movement - Fully serviced by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Includes a handmade leather watch pouch - Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Cartier Art Deco Pencil Watch with Concealed Lighter Sterling Silver
description: Cartier is one of the most revered makers of jewellery, watch and accessories. This particular item incorporates three features – it’s a pencil, a lighter and a watch! Imagine the guy who decided to go to Cartier to buy or order a pencil, with a watch plus a lighter to light up his cigars. Definitely an interesting and cool person! The pencil is made in sterling silver and remains in excellent condition. The top section of the pencil houses a small watch with manual wind movement, with winding crown to the right. The dial has Art Deco numerals and two blued hands. The lighter sits below the watch. The pencil comes in its original Cartier box and our watchmaker has fully serviced the movement. We love Cartier and enjoy sharing our knowledge and passion with you guys. Let’s talk! Specifications: - Cartier Pencil Watch - Sterling silver - Dates to the 1930s - White dial with painted Art Deco numerals - Blue steeled hands - Calibre FHF1 manual wind movement - Fully serviced by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Includes a handmade leather watch pouch - Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Rolex Oyster Perpetual Date Black Dial Silver Print "Upside Down" Ref 1500
description: Today we have a cool and interesting 34mm Oyster to share with you. We’ve called this the Upside Down because the reference number is between the lugs at the six o’clock end of the watch and the serial number is at 12, then opposite to normal and so upside down! The 34mm Oyster is so much great value and fun and don’t be fooled by its size; it packs a big punch. This OP Date is in great condition and houses a beautiful black dial with silver text and unusual door-stop markers at six and nine. We believe that the hands have been relumed at some point. The case remains strong all round and the bezel is also very clean. This cool OP Date comes on a vintage Oyster rivet bracelet reference 7205 with 57 endlinks and clasp stamped ‘2 66’. The watch, which has a slow-set date, has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. We would love to help you find your next beautiful watch. Please get in touch with us and let us know what you’re looking for! Specifications: - Reference 1500 - 34mm steel case - Reversed serial and reference numbers - Serial 144xxxx dating to 1966 - Black dial with silver text - Oyster rivet bracelet 7205 with 57 endlinks - Fully serviced by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Tissot Navigator Chronograph Lemania Movement Ref 817
description: We love this Type XX style chronograph by Tissot. Featuring a rotating bezel for measuring elapsed time and a black dial with bold luminous Arabic hours, this watch has all the charm of a military chronograph that we know you guys love. And that bezel insert that has faded naturally of the decades just tops it off… Housed in a 40mm steel case the watch remains in really great minty condition with two pushers and a large winding crown, with a bi-directional bezel for measuring elapsed time. The bezel insert has faded beautifully over time to a ghost-like shade. The black dial has two sub dials and bold Arabic painted hours that have taken on a lovely patina. The dial has the so-called Box T logo and is signed T SWISS MADE T. This Tissot will come on the pictured Bulang and Sons strap. The Lemania calibre 827 manual-wind movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. This is a great looking and very rare chrono from a well-established brand. Please get in touch to start a conversation! Specifications: - Navigator Chronograph Ref 817 - 40mm steel case - Dates to late 1960s - Black dial with painted Arabic hours - Faded bezel insert - Lemania calibre 827 manual wind movement - Movement serial 250xxxx - Pictured Bulang and Sons strap - Fully serviced by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Longines "Chronostop" Ref 5824
description: They say big is beautiful…well, it’s certainly true in this case! This is a very Collectible Longines monopusher watch with all the hallmarks of the brand’s dedication to offering interesting pilots’ watches in the mid-20th century, as demonstrated by the bold Arabic hours and large onion winding crown. The steel case measures a huge 47.5mm and has a single chronograph pusher. The black dial has luminous Arabic numerals, an inner rotating bezel calibrated to 60 minutes and two “running seconds” hands - one for counting seconds and the other one is actually a minute counter. The watch will come on the pictured Nato strap. The movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. The extract states that this watch was invoiced on 31 March 1951 to the company Knyath, which was at that time the agent for Israel. This is a super cool and still under the radar watch that we’re sure you guys will love. Please arrange a visit to our lounge or a call to discuss this lovely Longines some more! Specifications: - Longines Chronostop ref 5824 - 47.5mm case - Mono-pusher chronograph with large onion crown - Case number 2x - Black dial with painted hours and inner bezel - Calibre 1268Z stop second movement - Movement number 764xxxx dating to the early 1950s - With Longines Extract from the Archives - Comes on the pictured Nato strap - Fully serviced by our watchmaker - One-year mechanical warranty - Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Rolex Oyster Precision 6426 Silver Dial FAP Issued Box and Papers
description: This is a very cool but normal looking manual wind Oyster in the classic 34mm Oyster case. The watch was, however, issued to the Fuerza Aérea Peru, or FAP as collectors refer to it, the Peruvian Air Force. This military issued Oyster is the ultimate in stealth cool! The silver soleil dial has applied hour makers and coronet and has matching hands. The case back features the Fuerza Aérea Peru engraving and it also has the last three digits of the serial inside the case back, as you would hope to see with a special delivery watch. The watch also comes with the original guarantee paper,(..in Spanish language !) box and hang tag. The watch comes on a rivet Oyster bracelet reference 7205 in nice condition and has ref 57 endlinks and a clasp stamped ‘1 71’. This reference 6426 houses a manual wind movement that has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. ‌This is a lovely watch with an incredible history…exactly what we love at Bulang and Sons. Please get in touch to discuss any details and let’s do a deal. The dial is in good condition too and the radium hour markers have taken on a deep patina, as has the lume in the broad arrow hands. title: Omega Railmaster ref 135004-63
description: Off the beaten path is a cool place to explore sometimes. Finding interesting and unusual vintage tool watches is something we love to do and offer to you guys. This one is very cool! This is an early example of the Railmaster, Omega’s professional series watch for those operating in environments of high magnetic fields. It’s the equivalent of the Rolex Milgauss or the IWC Ingenieur. Dating to 1963, the watch has a 38mm steel case, with extra thick dial (1mm thick instead of the usual 0.4mm) and special antimagnetic inner case back and the case back features a hippocampus. This Railmaster’s dial is in lovely original condition with stunning patina on the hour markers and crisp white text. The manual wind Calibre 286 movement has been fully checked and regulated by our watchmaker. Do you love this watch or are you looking for something similar? If so, get in touch and let us help you find your next beautiful timepiece. title: Tudor Black Bay Black ETA-Movement Full Set Ref 79220N
description: The Black Bay is one of the most successful modern watch lines and has been something of a phenomenon with both new and vintage collectors. The Black Bay Black was introduced in late 2014 but was made with an ETA movement for only a few weeks before being replaced by the in-house movement version. This makes it one of the rarest modern Tudors and a sure-fire future collectible. This watch remains in great condition and the steel case is excellent as are the black dial and bezel insert with cool red-triangle detail. These dials are clue about the movement beneath as they have the early Tudor Sub style “smiley face” text at the bottom of the dial and are not COSC rated. The watch comes on a steel three-link bracelet reference 95740 with solid endlinks. Included are both the inner and outer boxes, two booklets and the guarantee card dated 2016. The watch has been checked and regulated by our watchmaker. We love these watches and are delighted to be offering it for sale. Get in touch to do a deal and be ready for summer! title: Patek Philippe Calatrava ref. 5026-011P Black Dial in Platinum Millennium limited Edition
description: There is something quietly reassuring about the design language of the Patek Philippe Calatrava—a sense of balance and refinement that has remained largely untouched since the days of the original reference 96. The reference 5026 carries that same spirit, yet with a subtle evolution. It builds upon everything we have come to appreciate about the Calatrava: clean lines, elegant proportions, and a dial that feels both timeless and effortless. At the same time, it introduces a gentle nod to another great name in watchmaking. The interplay between Patek Philippe’s restrained design and the influence of Breguet creates something truly special—an aesthetic that feels familiar, yet just a little different. This particular example, however, tells an even more interesting story. Created as a Millennium edition in the year 2000 and limited to just 130 pieces, it steps slightly away from the expected. Instead of the more commonly seen Arabic numerals, the dial features painted Roman numerals at the quarter hours, balanced by simple stick indices for the remaining markers. It’s a subtle variation, but one that gives the watch its own quiet identity. Inside, the beautifully refined cal. 240 micro-rotor movement—first introduced in 1977—keeps things slim and elegant. The off-centre seconds subdial adds a touch of asymmetry, while the modest 33.5 mm case ensures it wears exactly as a classic Calatrava should: understated and close to the wrist. Produced between 1997 and 2002, the reference 5026 remains one of those pieces that doesn’t seek attention, but rewards those who take the time to look a little closer. This fine Calatrava is fitted with a new Patek leather strap with its original platinum folding clasp fitted and the watch and its automatic movement with its cal 240 has been fully serviced by our watch maker. For those who appreciate the quiet elegance of Patek Philippe ,get in touch to discuss this Millennium Calatrava today! Specifications: Patek Philippe Calatrava Ref. 5026-011 Millennium limited edition 33.5mm Platinum case with display caseback Movement serial 315xxxx dating to the year 2000 Black dial with painted Roman numeral hour markers Limited edition of 130 pieces White stick hands and small seconds sub dial Automatic movement Cal. 240 with gold micro rotor Pictured Patek Philippe leather strap with original PT Patek folding clasp fitted Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Rolex Submariner Date Ref. 1680 “Libyan MilSub”
description: We are happy to offer one of the few known examples of the Libyan MilSub 1680. Military Submariners are among the most sought-after watches, and this is one of the rarest—we’ve seen fewer than a handful. The case remains in excellent condition and, importantly, the inside features the full serial number engraved in the correct, accepted font. This was done by Rolex in Geneva for special-order watches such as MilSubs, Comex pieces, and other important references. The case back bears an Arabic inscription that translates to: “Armed Forces Military Shop.” The watch is fitted with a matte Mk2 dial in great condition, featuring a lovely yellow patina on both the hour markers and hands. It also boasts a beautifully faded blue bezel insert that adds vintage character and charm. We received the watch fitted with a non-cyclops plexi crystal—and since we love the look, we decided to leave it just as it is! The solid link Oyster bracelet (reference 93150) is in very good condition, with a clasp stamped ‘S 9’ and 580 end links. The watch has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. We love vintage Rolex; it’s one of the cornerstones of Bulang and Sons. We would love to discuss this watch in more detail with you and share our passion. Get in touch and let’s talk. title: Breitling Navitimer Black Dial”Twin jet” Ref 7806
description: The vintage Breitling Navitimer is one of the most iconic watches, as recognisable as a Speedmaster or a Submariner! Breitling launched a new manual-wind chrono as a successor to the classic Navitimer 806 in 1972. Reference 7806 was housed in the traditional 41mm steel case and powered by the, following the end of the Venus calibre 178 supply. The revised Navitimer 7806 was distinguished by its prominent Big Eyes hour and minute counters, a running seconds display at 12, and, for the first time, a date feature at 4:30. This is a lovely example with a 41mm steel case is in outstanding condition and the bezel is still well defined. The watch houses a black dial with twin plane variation with silver printed logo. This cool Navigator will come on the pictured Bulang and Sons leather strap with original Breitling steel buckle fitted. The manual-wind Valjoux calibre R7740 movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. Iconic chronos are never out of style and this Breitling is a great example. Please arrange a visit to us in our lounge or let’s jump on a video call. title: Rolex Day-Date 1807 Linen Sigma Dial Bark Finish in 18k Yellow Gold
description: The Rolex Day-Date, introduced in 1956, made horological history as the first waterproof, self-winding chronometer to display both the date and the full day of the week spelled out on the dial. Produced exclusively in precious metals, it has always been crafted only in 18k gold or platinum. For its debut, Rolex created a distinctive three-piece bracelet with semi-circular links, officially named the President bracelet. The watch became closely associated with global power after Lyndon B. Johnson was often seen wearing a yellow gold example during the 1960s. Rolex later embraced this connection, promoting it as “the watch worn by presidents.” This particular Day-Date features a champagne linen-effect dial with unusual hour markers and Sigma markings. While preserving the classic character of the watch, these details add a unique and distinctive touch. The luminous plots and hand lume have aged beautifully, developing a very pleasing patina over time. The day disc is displayed in Spanish. The watch is fitted with a non-concealed clasp ( stamped ‘4 72’) President bracelet in excellent condition, suitable for wrists up to approximately 18.5 cm, and featuring the correct and rarely seen “Bark” hand-finishing. The slow-set movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. These Linen dials are beautiful , bark finish is something rare and charming ,let us get you this bad boy! Specifications: Rolex Day-Date Reference 1807 36mm yellow gold case, plexi-crystal, Bark finish Serial 338xxxx dating to 1973 Champagne linen-finish pie-pan dial with sigma markings Spanish date disc Yellow gold President bracelet , with Bark finish Slow setting movement Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Audemars Piguet Millenary Chronograph Black Dial ref. 25822ST with Original Certificate of Origine
description: The Royal-Oak may be Audemars Piguet’s most iconic creation, but the Millenary has steadily earned its place as one of the brand’s most distinctive and instantly recognizable designs since its debut. With its oval case and off-centered dial layout, it may not be the first AP that comes to mind—but it is undoubtedly one of the most intriguing. A bit of history on the Millenary: the concept for its horizontally oval case traces back to a 1951 Audemars Piguet design that never gained traction at the time and was eventually shelved. Decades later, the idea was revived and refined, leading to the launch of the Millenary collection in 1995—named in celebration of the approaching third millennium. On offer here is reference 25822ST, a steel chronograph measuring 41 × 37 mm, featuring a blue cabochon crown and two pushers on the right side for chronograph operation. It presents a striking black pie-pan–style dial with white printed text, a telemeter scale, and a base-1000 tachymeter. Three sub-dials serve the chronograph functions, complemented by a date window that balances the layout—complete with a magnifier integrated into the inside of the crystal. Oversized Arabic numerals act as hour markers, further enhancing the watch’s bold and unconventional character. The watch is fitted with the pictured leather strap and comes equipped with a stylish steel AP folding clasp. Furthermore, the watch comes with its original certificate of origin, stating a sale in Belgium. The watch is powered by the automatic Caliber 2226/2840, which has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. Enquire now to secure this distinctive and quirky Millenary ! Specifications: Audemars Piguet Millenary Chronograph reference 25822ST 41x36 mm, steel case in oval shape Case number E44xxx, dating to the second half of the 1990’s Black dial with sub dials , white hands Automatic Caliber 2226/2840 Comes on the pictured leather strap with original AP folding clasp Original certificate of origin, stating a sale in Belgium. Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Patek Philippe Calatrava 3796J Silver Dial in 18k Yellow Gold
description: This Patek Philippe Ref. 3796, launched in 1982 and produced until the late 1990s, is the direct neo-vintage successor to the original 1932 Calatrava Ref. 96. It faithfully maintains the classic 31mm case size, tapered lugs, and Bauhaus-inspired design of the iconic Ref. 96. We might regard this as the last “classic-sized” Calatrava that remained entirely faithful to the proportions of the inaugural model. The 18k yellow gold case is in excellent condition and features short, elegant lugs, a snap-back case back, and a small winding crown. The dial is a classic silver with a soft brushed finish, a “dot-dot” minute track, boat-shaped elegant hour markers, and a classic small running seconds at six o’clock, topped off with sharp dauphine hands. It is paired with an original Patek Philippe leather strap and an 18k yellow gold Patek Philippe pin buckle. The watch and its hand-wound Cal. 215 movement have been fully serviced by our watchmaker and are ready for many years to come. A timeless Calatrava for the true purist. Contact us to secure this piece! Specifications: Patek Philippe Calatrava Reference 3796 31mm 18k yellow gold case with snap back case back Silver dial with applied hour markers and non-lumed dauphine hands Patek leather strap with Patek Philippe 18k gold pin buckle Cal. 215 hand wound movement Case number no 280xxxx/movement no 135xxxx dating to the 1980’s Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide FedEx Priority shipping

title: Rolex Explorer 114270 with Original Guarantee Paper
description: Launched in 1953 alongside another legend, the Rolex Submariner, the Rolex Explorer set the tone for what a proper tool watch should be. Built with mountaineering in mind—think Himalayan expeditions—it was all about legibility, robustness, and reliability. (And yes, the Rolex Turn-O-Graph made its debut that same year as well.) Since then, the Explorer has built a reputation as one of the most versatile watches out there. The kind of piece that can genuinely be your only watch—and still feel right in almost any situation. While the model has seen its share of updates over the decades, the core formula hasn’t really changed: steel Oyster case, black dial, Arabic numerals at 3, 6, and 9, and no date. Simple, functional, and exactly the point. At hand we have a reference 114270, produced from 2001 to 2010 as the refined successor to the 14270. The classic 36mm case is in excellent condition, paired with a smooth bezel and a strong Oyster bracelet (ref. 78690) with solid end links and a clasp stamped “CL2.” The watch comes with its original Rolex punched guarantee paper, confirming a sale in Singapore in 2004—always a nice detail. It has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready for many more years on the wrist. No fuss, no noise—just a great Explorer. Get in touch. title: Rolex Oyster Perpetual "Bubble Back" Silver Dial in steel ref. 2764
description: This early Rolex Oyster Perpetual reference 2764 was introduced in the late 1930s and features a distinctive domed case back, designed to accommodate one of Rolex’s early automatic movements. Often referred to as “ovetti” (little eggs) in Italy, these watches are an underrated vintage gem on the wrist—full of charm and character. And while the 32mm size might sound modest, they actually wear larger thanks to the thicker, more pronounced case back. The case is in excellent condition, featuring hooded lugs, single-sided lug holes, and a smooth bezel—clean and well-preserved throughout. The dial is a silver, no-lume execution with simple hour markers, a small seconds subdial at six o’clock, and crisp black printed “chronomètre” text below the handset. The leaf hands are also without lume, keeping the look balanced and period-correct. We’ve paired this “little egg” with one of our straight end-link beads-of-rice bracelets—an ideal match that really completes the vintage look on the wrist. The watch and its automatic movement have been fully serviced by our watchmaker and are ready to wear. The “little egg” that wears way bigger—don’t miss it! Specifications: Rolex Reference 2764 “Bubble back “ 32mm steel case with one side lug wholes Serial 107xxx dating to 1940 No lume silver dial, signed “chronomètre” with small second Leaf style no lume hands Pictured steel straight end links bracelet Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Rolex Oysterquartz Datejust 17000 White Buckley Dial with Papers
description: The Rolex Oysterquartz Datejust tells a slightly different story within the world of Rolex—one shaped by a time of change and innovation. Introduced in 1977, during the height of the Quartz Crisis, it marked Rolex’s answer to a rapidly evolving watch industry, blending precision with a distinctly modern design language! There is something unmistakably “of its time” about the Oysterquartz. Its angular case and integrated bracelet give it that characteristic 1970s aesthetic—often compared to the work of Gérald Genta—yet still unmistakably Rolex in execution. A design that feels bold, a little different, and perhaps more relevant today than ever. The present example, reference 17000, showcases this beautifully. The case remains strong, paired with a smooth bezel and fitted with its correct 17000B Oyster bracelet, finished with a clasp stamped “GC.” On the wrist, it has a presence that is both confident and refined. The white dial—often referred to as a “Buckley” dial—adds a charming twist. Black painted Roman numerals, matching black hands, and softly aged cream lume plots create a pleasing contrast, giving the watch a touch of character without losing its clean appearance. Accompanying the watch are its original and seldom-seen Argentinian warranty papers, noting a sale in 1990, along with the Rolex Oysterquartz booklet. Fully serviced by our watchmaker, and fitted with a fresh battery, it is ready to be worn and enjoyed for years to come. A slightly different side of Rolex—one that stands apart, yet fits in effortlessly, get in touch with us to talk 'Quartz ! title: Rolex Day-Date 1803 Olive Grey Buckley Dial in 18k Yellow Gold
description: The Rolex Day-Date 1803 is already one of the great classics of vintage Rolex collecting, but this 1971 example is something else entirely. What makes it so special is its exceptionally rare olive green to warm grey Buckley dial — a dial type defined by its painted Roman numerals and named after legendary New York dealer John Buckley, whose fondness for these unusual dials helped make them so collectible in the first place. What we love here most is the colour. In the metal, this olive greyish green to warm grey tone has an incredible warmth and depth to it, and against the 18k yellow gold case it becomes even more striking. White Buckley dials do surface from time to time, but coloured examples are in a very different category altogether. We have only seen this dial colour a handful of times in real life, and that alone says everything — this is an exceptionally unusual and deeply attractive Day-Date. Adding even more character is the yellow gold rivet-link Oyster bracelet. Most Day-Dates are seen on a President bracelet, so this sportier bracelet changes the whole feel of the watch and gives it a far more individual look. That combination — rare coloured Buckley dial, warm yellow gold case and sporty rivet bracelet — is what makes this watch so incredibly compelling to us. It feels refined, but also quirky, confident and full of personality. Condition-wise, the watch is just as impressive. The case remains beautifully strong and full, the fluted bezel is sharp, and the dial is perfect, with no blemishes or damage. These are exactly the details that matter on a watch like this. This Day-Date has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is fitted with an 18k yellow gold Oyster rivet bracelet, stamped 2/65, with 76 end links. This is the kind of vintage Rolex that seasoned collectors rarely get the chance to encounter. If you would like to know more about this exceptional and highly unusual Day-Date, please contact our experts — we would be delighted to assist you further. Specifications: Rolex Day-Date reference 1803 36mm yellow gold case Serial 276xxxx dating to 1971 Olive Grey Buckley Dial 18k Yellow gold riveted link oyster bracelet Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Rolex Day-Date 1803/9 Silver Dial in 18k White Gold
description: The Rolex Day-Date has one of the richest and most recognizable histories in watchmaking—quietly evolving while always staying true to its original idea: ultimate everyday luxury. Presented here is a wonderfully understated example in 18k white gold. A somewhat more discreet take on the Day-Date, it carries all the presence of the model, yet in a more subtle and “stealthy” execution. The classic 36 mm case remains in great condition, accompanied by a crisp fluted bezel that catches the light just as it should. The silver dial, finished with a soft soleil texture, offers a beautiful sense of depth. Thin stick hour markers and clean black printed text keep things elegant and balanced, while the gently aged lume plots have developed a warm beige tone—adding just the right amount of contrast and character. Fitted with an English day disc, the watch has been paired with one of our Bulang & Sons leather straps, a combination we feel complements its refined appearance perfectly. The watch has been fully serviced by our watchmaker, and its slow-set movement is running as it should—ready to be enjoyed for many years to come. A quiet expression of luxury, for those who prefer their statement to remain understated! title: Rolex Day-Date 18038 Linen Champagne Dial in 18k Yellow Gold
description: The Rolex Day-Date, introduced in 1956, was a true milestone in watchmaking—the first waterproof, self-winding chronometer to display both the date and the day of the week spelled out in full. From the very beginning, it has represented Rolex at its most refined, crafted exclusively in 18k gold or platinum. With the introduction of the reference 18038 in 1977, Rolex brought a practical update to the model with the single quickset function, allowing the date to be adjusted with ease—an everyday improvement that made a noticeable difference in wearability. This particular example stands out thanks to its beautiful champagne-coloured linen dial. The textured surface plays gently with the light, giving the watch a quiet yet confident presence on the wrist. The case remains strong, with a fluted bezel that still shows crisp and well-defined edges. A German day disc adds a subtle touch of individuality. These five-digit Day-Dates are especially appealing, as they retain the classic 36 mm proportions and elegant character of the earlier models, while benefiting from the durability of a sapphire crystal—making them perfectly suited for regular wear. The President bracelet, fitted with a concealed clasp, remains in very fine and tight condition and will comfortably fit a wrist up to approximately 18 cm. The movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is running as it should. A timeless expression of refinement and quiet confidence—ready to be worn and enjoyed. We would be delighted to welcome you to our lounge or to discuss this piece with you personally, whenever it suits you. Specifications: Rolex Day-Date reference 18038 36mm yellow 18k yellow gold case Rolex Serial 6214xxx dating to 1979 Champagne coloured Linen texture dial German day wheel Yellow gold President bracelet with concealed clasp Single quick-setting movement Fully serviced by our watchmaker One-year mechanical warranty Free worldwide Fedex Priority shipping

title: Rolex Datejust 1603 Black Sigma Dial
description: We really love these cool and classy Datejusts – they are never out of style and there is never a ‘wrong’ occasion to wear one of these fantastic watches. We have found another nice example to share with all you. The case is even with nice thick lugs and the steel engine turned bezel is still well defined. The black pie -pan dial has a matte finish with crisp text, sigma markings ( denoting the white gold markers on the dial !) and stick markers, with a bit unusual fatter markers at 6 and 9 o’clock. The hands are later to the watch as the lume is luminova .The folded type Jubilee bracelet, reference 6215H, is in nice condition with a clasp stamped ‘2 71’ and reference 55 end links. The slow-set movement has been fully serviced by our watchmaker. We always try and keep a good stock of cool DJs. Please get in touch and help us find your perfect one! title: Rolex Day-date Tridor Peach Diamond Marker Dial  with Original Guarantee Paper ref 18239B
description: Rolex is not known for being a particularly “experimental” company. That said, every now and then they stepped a little over the edge—one great example being the Tridor models. Introduced in the late 1980s and produced through the 1990s, the Rolex Tridor (“three gold”) was offered only on the Day-Date and Lady-Datejust models. These watches never truly took off at the time, but today they have become highly collectible and increasingly appreciated by enthusiasts. At hand, we have a classic 36mm Day-Date, reference 18239B, with the “B” denoting its three-color gold construction. The watch features a white gold case and crown, paired with an 18k yellow gold fluted bezel. The iconic President bracelet is executed with 18k white gold outer links, while the center link consists of alternating bands of rose, yellow, and white gold. The case is in excellent condition, as is the fluted bezel, and the case back still partially retains its original green sticker. The President bracelet, reference 8285, is in great condition, features a concealed clasp, and currently fits wrists up to approximately 18–18.5 cm. The dial is a super-cool, almost “peach” color—definitely not your standard champagne tone—and is tastefully set with brilliant-cut diamonds and alternating baguette-cut diamonds. Completing the look is an English-language day disc. This unusual and distinctive Day-Date comes with its original punched warranty paper, showing a sale date in 1991. The watch, including its double quick-set movement, has been fully serviced by our watchmaker and is ready to be enjoyed for many years to come. If you’re looking for a Day-Date that breaks away from the norm and embraces Rolex’s rare experimental side, this Tridor is a standout. Curious about this three-gold icon? Let’s talk and get it on your wrist. title: Patek Philippe Calatrava 3998J Argenté Dial ref. 3998j with Original Certificate of Origine
description: The Patek Philippe Calatrava is widely regarded as one of the most timeless and elegant wristwatches ever created. This reference 3998J in 18k yellow gold perfectly embodies that classic design, featuring a clean warm silver vertical-brushed dial without lume, a simple three-hand configuration, and a screw-down case back. At 34 mm, it strikes the ideal balance between understated sophistication and presence. The silvered (or “argenté,” in Patek’s terminology) dial is in excellent condition, with a distinctive “dot-dot” applied minute track, crisp black text, and applied hour markers, all paired with beautifully sharp dauphine hands. The watch comes with its original Certificate of Origin, confirming a 1992 sale in Germany, as well as the original Patek paper holder. This refined Calatrava is fitted with a handsome Bulang & Sons strap, complemented by an original 18k gold Patek pin buckle. Powered by the automatic Calibre 315, the watch has been fully serviced and is ready to be enjoyed. A reference for the discerning collector—contact us for details !
